Title: Data Analyst
Description:
VOY is a fast growing Healthtech scale-up, aiming to be the healthcare provider that millions of people trust as their partner to a long and healthy life. Our platform allows users to quickly and safely diagnose possible conditions, get treatment and support them afterwards to live a higher quality life. To do this we are building an all in one healthcare platform which brings together world leading medical expertise and technical excellence.

We are looking for a talented Data Analyst to join our growing Data team and help drive smarter, data-informed decisions across the organisation.

The Opportunity

This is a high-impact role where the insights you surface and the dashboards you build directly influence how our teams operate and how well we support our patients.

Who you are


You have strong SQL skills and can write queries confidently to extract, transform and analyse data
You're comfortable with data visualisation tools and understand how to design dashboards that are intuitive and actionable
You think carefully about data -what metrics mean, what the data is actually telling you, and how to communicate it clearly
You're curious and self-driven, comfortable exploring datasets to uncover insights and answer difficult questions
You're a natural communicator who can translate analytical findings into clear, actionable recommendations
You collaborate well with both technical and non-technical stakeholders and take ownership end-to-end
You're excited about working in a fast-paced environment and motivated by the idea that your work helps improve patient outcomes at scale


What you will do

Translate data into insight


Conduct ad-hoc analyses to answer business questions from stakeholders across Operations, Finance, Clinical and Programme teams
Identify trends, patterns and anomalies in operational and clinical data that impact service delivery and patient outcomes
Present findings in clear, compelling ways that drive decision-making across the organisation


Build and maintain dashboards & reports


Design and build dashboards in Looker that track key metrics across the business - from programme delivery KPIs to financial performance
Develop automated reports that keep stakeholders informed without requiring manual updates
Ensure dashboards are accurate, performant and easy to navigate for end users


Contribute to data quality & modelling


Write clear, optimised SQL to query and manipulate data reliably
Collaborate with the Data Engineering team on data modelling, dbt transformations and data governance
Identify and escalate data quality issues and help ensure consistency across systems
Help define and track metrics that matter, ensuring every KPI is well understood and correctly calculated


Partner across teams


Work closely with stakeholders across Operations, Clinical, Finance and Programme Delivery to understand their data needs
Communicate complex data concepts in a way that's accessible to non-technical stakeholders
Help drive adoption of data-driven thinking and self-serve analytics across the organisation


What we need from you


2-4 years of experience in a data analyst, business analyst or similar analytics role
Proficiency in SQL - you can write queries to explore, filter, join and aggregate data reliably
Experience with at least one BI/visualisation tool (Looker, Tableau, Power BI, or similar)
Familiarity with modern data stacks and tools (BigQuery, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, dbt are all helpful)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with an eye for detail
Excellent communication skills - able to explain complex findings clearly to diverse audiences
This is a hybrid role requiring 3 days per week in the office


Experience you may have


A degree in a quantitative subject (Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, etc.)
Experience in healthcare, public health or regulated industries
Exposure to Python or R for data analysis and statistical work
Experience working in fast-growing, high-impact environments
Familiarity with dbt or other data transformation frameworks


Please note that this is a hybrid role with a requirement of in office presence, 3 days per week

Healthcare is broken. We’re fixing it — and we need your help.

Back in 2019, we launched MANUAL to tackle men's health (starting with hair loss and sexual health). A few years later, we launched Voy with a focus on weight loss. But our ambition has always been bigger. Healthcare too often treats conditions in isolation, not people as a whole, and we're on a mission to prove there's a better way.

In 2025, we evolved from MANUAL into Voy, a brand built for the long game: supporting people through every stage of life, not just a single diagnosis. Today, we support over 1 million members across the UK, Germany and Brazil, delivering personalised care across weight loss, menopause, hair loss and TRT (with more categories on the way).

Ready to join us and change how healthcare feels for millions of people?

Title: HOLT Data Analyst
Description:
Your role

This Analyst role sits within the HOLT team in London and provides broad exposure to HOLT’s valuation framework by collaborating across the Customs and Data Solutions, Model Development and Investment Strategy teams.

The role is designed for an early‑career professional with strong analytical fundamentals who is keen to develop across multiple areas, rather than specialize immediately.

The Analyst will support senior team members across projects spanning research, data analysis, portfolio analytics, impacting client deliverables, while building a strong understanding of how HOLT insights are developed and applied across UBS.


Support valuation and financial analysis using the proprietary HOLT methodology across companies and sectors
Collaborate with senior colleagues to support the development of client solutions using HOLT tools and Excel based models
Provide structured financial and data analysis to support research, investment strategies, and client deliverables
Support the preparation, maintenance, and quality assurance of HOLT fundamental datasets and analytical outputs
Contribute to the analysis and ongoing monitoring of equity investment strategies and portfolio analytics
Assist in delivering standard and bespoke analytical outputs for internal stakeholders and external clients
Support documentation, testing, and process improvements across models, data, and analytical tools
Build a working understanding of how HOLT insights are applied across research, investment products, and client workflows


Job Type

Full Time

Job Reference #

337395BR

City

London

Your team

HOLT is a proprietary valuation and analytics platform within UBS, supporting research, investment products, and client solutions across the firm.

This Analyst role offers structured exposure to multiple HOLT capabilities, providing a strong foundation for future development within HOLT or the broader Investment Bank.

The role emphasizes learning, collaboration, and practical application of valuation and data insights, making it an ideal entry point for candidates seeking a broad and flexible early‑career path.

Your expertise


2–4 years of relevant experience in finance, analytics, research, data, or a related role
Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Engineering, Computer Science, or similar
Good understanding of financial statements, corporate valuation, and equity markets
Strong programming fundamentals with a focus on automation and problem solving;
Ability to learn and adapt quickly
Clear written and verbal communication skills
Collaborative mindset with a willingness to learn across different functional areas
Demonstrate a passion for financial markets and investment decision‑making
Progress toward CFA or similar professional qualification
Prior exposure to equity research, investment strategies, or data/analytics roles
Familiar with Databricks; AI code assistants; NLP and Machine Learning; LLMs and AI Coding tools


About Us
UBS is a leading and truly global wealth manager and the leading universal bank in Switzerland. We also provide diversified asset management solutions and focused investment banking capabilities. Headquartered in Zurich, Switzerland, UBS is present in more than 50 markets around the globe.

We know that great work is never done alone. That’s why we place collaboration at the heart of everything we do. Because together, we’re more than ourselves. Want to find out more? Visit ubs.com/careers.

Join us

At UBS, we know that it's our people, with their diverse skills, experiences and backgrounds, who drive our ongoing success. We’re dedicated to our craft and passionate about putting our people first, with new challenges, a supportive team, opportunities to grow and flexible working options when possible. Our inclusive culture brings out the best in our employees, wherever they are on their career journey. And we use artificial intelligence (AI) to work smarter and more efficiently. We also recognize that great work is never done alone. That’s why collaboration is at the heart of everything we do. Because together, we’re more than ourselves.

We’re committed to disability inclusion and if you need reasonable accommodation/adjustments throughout our recruitment process, you can always contact us.

Disclaimer / Policy statements

UBS is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We respect and seek to empower each individual and support the diverse cultures, perspectives, skills and experiences within our workforce.

Title: CRM Data Analyst
Description:
INTRODUCTION

At Burberry, we believe creativity opens spaces. Our purpose is to unlock the power of imagination to push boundaries and open new possibilities for our people, our customers and our communities. This is the core belief that has guided Burberry since it was founded in 1856 and is central to how we operate as a company today.




We aim to provide an environment for creative minds from different backgrounds to thrive, bringing a wide range of skills and experiences to everything we do. As a purposeful, values-driven brand, we are committed to being a force for good in the world as well, creating the next generation of sustainable luxury for customers, driving industry change and championing our communities.




JOB PURPOSE

We are seeking a highly analytical and technically skilled CRM Data Analyst to join us on a FTC to join our growing Global Customer team. In this role, you will leverage data to drive meaningful customer engagement, optimize CRM strategies, and support the brand’s ambition to deliver exceptional client experiences.




You will work closely with CRM, Data Science, and Digital teams to translate data into actionable insights, using advanced analytics tools and coding in Databricks to structure and analyse large datasets. The ideal candidate has a balance of technical expertise and business acumen, with a passion for the luxury and fashion industry.




RESPONSIBILITIES

• Build, maintain, and optimize customer data models and segmentation frameworks in Databricks

• Identify customer insights and trends that inform personalization, targeting, and strategic decision-making.

• Design and maintain interactive dashboards (preferably using Tableau and/or Looker and/or PowerBI), ensuring data accuracy and usability for key stakeholders

• Ensure data governance and consistency across global CRM and analytics platforms

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support omnichannel initiatives and clienteling tools, particularly with Data Science




• Update weekly, monthly, and quarterly reports to share with top management, providing insights and recommendations to inform strategic decision

• Perform ad hoc customer analyses to support the business in accelerating key customer metrics, particularly retention and purchase frequency

• Partner with CRM and Marketing teams to measure One to One and One to Many Campaign effectiveness, looking at Global and Regional performance

• Support regional teams by providing training on CRM tools, dashboards, and best practices, as well as performing ad hoc analyses to help them drive local customer engagement




PERSONAL PROFILE

5+ years of experience in CRM Analytics, Data Analytics, or related roles, ideally within the fashion, retail, or luxury sector

Strong proficiency in Databricks (SQL, Python, or PySpark) for data transformation and analysis

Solid understanding of CRM principles, customer segmentation, and campaign measurement.

Experience working with customer databases, CDPs, and marketing automation systems (e.g., Salesforce, Braze)

Proven experience creating and maintaining reports and dashboards in Tableau and Looker

Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail, with the ability to interpret complex data into clear business insights

Excellent communication skills and stakeholder management, with the ability to present findings to non-technical audiences

A passion for fashion, luxury, and customer experience




Burberry is an Equal Opportunities Employer and as such, treats all applications equally and recruits purely on the basis of skills and experience.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Senior Data Analyst (Contract) £500–£700 per day | Outside IR35 | London




I'm partnering with a customer-focused retail and hospitality business that is looking to hire its first dedicated Senior Data Analyst. This is a unique opportunity to build and shape the organisation's data capability from the ground up, working directly with the senior leadership team to drive commercial decision-making across the business.




Initially, you'll work with data extracted from property management, point-of-sale and booking systems, developing reporting and analytics while laying the foundations for a scalable reporting environment.




Key Responsibilities

Develop customer segmentation
Analyse sales performance, including pipeline conversion, sales velocity and lead source effectiveness.
Establish consistent revenue definitions and reporting standards across multiple systems and business units.
Produce weekly executive trading reports and commercial performance dashboards.
Partner with Finance, Revenue Management, Marketing and Operations to deliver actionable insights and recommendations.
Identify opportunities to improve reporting, data quality and commercial decision-making across the business.
Help shape the organisation's future data and reporting capability as its first dedicated data specialist.




About You

You'll be an experienced Senior Data Analyst who is comfortable operating independently, engaging with senior stakeholders and translating complex data into clear commercial recommendations.




You'll ideally have:

Strong commercial analytics experience, ideally within hospitality, retail or another customer-centric business.
Excellent SQL and advanced Excel skills.
Experience with customer segmentation, CRM analytics and/or sales pipeline analysis.
A proven ability to communicate insights to senior leadership and influence commercial decisions.
Experience working with property management systems (PMS), point-of-sale systems or comparable operational platforms.
A proactive mindset with the ability to work in an evolving environment where you'll have the opportunity to build processes and influence the future direction of data.




This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced contractor who enjoys working closely with senior stakeholders, solving commercial challenges and making a visible impact within a growing business.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
The Company




We’re Octopus Legacy: one of the fastest growing tech companies in the country, part of the UK’s most exciting and best-trusted group (you may have heard of some of our sister companies…) and we have a mission.




We’re on a path to scale, aiming to become a household name while transforming a long-established industry and helping our customers prepare for, and find support after, death.




But we can’t do it alone, and that’s why we’re assembling a team of top performers to build with us.




Ready to be part of something big?




The Role

We’re looking for a Data Analyst to join our growing data team at Octopus Legacy. You’ll play a central role in helping teams across the business make smarter, faster, more informed decisions.




While you’ll sit within the data team, your day-to-day will involve working directly with stakeholders from across the organisation such as Growth, Product and Operations. You’ll turn messy real-world data into actionable insights, bring structure to ambiguity, and help the business better understand what drives performance.




This is a hands-on, high-impact role in a fast-moving environment. You’ll need to be comfortable navigating shifting priorities, championing a data culture, and bringing rigour to complexity.




We’re still early in our data journey - right now, the focus is on building the foundations: modelling key metrics, creating dashboards, and getting reliable data into the hands of the people who need it. As our data maturity grows, there’ll be opportunities to go deeper into analysis, experimentation, and more advanced analytics.




Key Responsibilities:

Act as a thought partner to teams across the business - helping them define problems, clarify questions, and uncover the metrics that matter
Translate stakeholder needs into end-to-end data solutions: from raw data to dbt models to dashboards in Metabase
Define and document clear, consistent metrics to support better decision-making across departments
Build intuitive, impactful dashboards that make data accessible and actionable for non-technical users
Deliver ad-hoc and project-based analysis to evaluate initiatives in growth, product, operations, and beyond
Collaborate with the data team to improve the reliability, clarity, and accessibility of our data models and pipelines
Operate with a high degree of autonomy - scoping projects, pushing for clarity, and challenging assumptions when needed
Contribute to a culture of curiosity and rigour, laying the groundwork for more advanced analytics and data science over time




Who Are You?

You have 2+ years of experience in a data analytics role, ideally in a startup or a company building out its data capability
You're proficient in SQL and have worked with BI tools (like Metabase, Looker, or Tableau) to build dashboards that help teams make better decisions
You have hands-on experience with dbt or similar tools, and a solid understanding of data modelling best practices
You’re comfortable working with messy or incomplete data, and know how to turn it into something clean, structured, and useful
You enjoy working with non-technical stakeholders - you ask thoughtful questions, clarify ambiguous requests, and help teams think through what they really need
You take ownership of your work, are comfortable scoping projects with limited direction, and aren’t afraid to challenge assumptions
You’re curious, pragmatic, and excited by the idea of shaping a modern data function from the ground up
Bonus: you have an interest in experimentation or data science, and are keen to grow into more advanced analytics over time




Skills and Knowledge




Must haves:

Strong curiosity and proactive mindset, with the ability to independently explore data, ask the right questions, identify opportunities, and drive projects forward without requiring constant guidance.
Experience in data analytics and the ability to extract actionable insights from complex datasets.
Proficiency with SQL, DBT, python is desirable.
Proven ability to work closely with business stakeholders and translate their needs into data-driven solutions.
Experience in developing reports and dashboards to monitor key metrics.
Strong communication skills, including the ability to present complex data in a way that is understandable and actionable for non-technical stakeholders across departments.
Proficiency with data visualisation tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Metabase).




Nice to haves/we can work on these:

Basic programming skills in languages such as Python.
Understanding of statistical methods and their application to business problems.
Experience working in a startup or fast-paced environment.




Bonus if you:

You have a basic understanding of Data Science concepts and are interested in learning more.
You have experience in training or educating non-technical teams on data literacy.




Our Mission

When people think about planning for death they think about wills, life insurance and funerals. We take these cold processes and turn them into something more human. Share more than money: leave voice notes, music, recipes. Shape a legacy that connects you while you're here. And after you're gone.




Founded by Sam after his mum died suddenly, we’re a group of people who work in death because we’ve been affected by it. We know the difference a good plan makes, and what it’s like when there isn’t one.




Death can come between us, leave mess, legal fees, frustration. But it can also make us stronger. We see a world where people talk openly about death, and work out the real meaning of legacy, one that connects to them. We’re here to make that world happen.




Benefits:

Octopus share incentive scheme.
Hybrid working (London Bridge).
Flexible holiday + extra day off for your Birthday.
Work from anywhere in the world for up to 4 weeks per year.
Vitality Health & Life Insurance.
Pension scheme.
Enhanced parental leave.
Free Will & LPAs + discounts on other Octopus services.
Cycle to Work Scheme and access to the Electric Vehicle (EV) Salary Sacrifice Scheme.
Octopus Giving: we match any charitable fundraising that you do up to £500.
Octopus Springboard: where we help our employees become fully-fledged entrepreneurs.
Dog friendly office.
Breakfast every day, snacks and wellness activities




We know that to be truly innovative, we need to have a diverse team around us. That is why Octopus Legacy is committed to creating an inclusive environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.

Title: Sustainability Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

The Royal Parks (TRP) is a charity created in March 2017. We manage over 5,000 acres of diverse parkland, rare habitats and historic buildings and monuments in eight Royal Parks across London. These are Hyde Park, Kensington Gardens, The Green Park, St James’s Park, The Regent's Park and Primrose Hill, Greenwich Park, Richmond Park and Bushy Park.

We also manage other important public spaces including Brompton Cemetery and Victoria Tower Gardens. Our eight Royal Parks and other iconic green spaces are among the most visited attractions in the UK with tens of millions of visits every year.

We are now looking for a Sustainability Data Analyst to join us on a full-time, permanent basis working 36 hours per week.

The Benefits

Salary of £43,000 - £48,000 per annum, depending on experience
26 days’ annual leave plus public holidays, increasing to 29 days after 3 years’ service
Pension scheme (3% employee contribution; up to 10% employer contribution)
Hybrid/agile working options
Private medical insurance and healthcare cash plan
Employee assistance programme and access to mental health first aiders
Learning and development opportunities
Cycle to work scheme
Offices in a beautiful location

The Role

We’ve recently launched our ambitious Sustainability Strategy (2025–2030), with a commitment to achieve Operational Net Zero by 2030 and Net Zero by 2050.

As the Sustainability Data Analyst, you will play a central role in delivering our strategy and driving meaningful environmental impact across our parks.

You’ll lead on analysing, managing and reporting sustainability data, helping us measure progress, improve data quality, and generate insights that shape decision-making across the organisation.

This is a highly collaborative role where you’ll work with internal teams, suppliers and contractors to strengthen reporting and embed best practice.

Additionally, you will:

Initiate meetings and briefing sessions with location managers and their colleagues and contractors as required.
Developing relationships with key industry professionals.

About You

To be considered as our Sustainability Data Analyst, you will need:

Experience analysing and managing sustainability or carbon data
Knowledge of carbon accounting (Scope 1–3) and reporting standards (e.g. GHG Protocol)
Strong data analysis and reporting skills (e.g. dashboards, Excel)
The ability to communicate complex data clearly to different audiences
Experience working with stakeholders, ideally including suppliers or procurement

We want to put everyone in the best possible position to succeed so if you think that you may need more support to complete our application process, please do get in touch.

The Royal Parks is strongly committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace and is an equal opportunities employer. We value diversity and encourage applications from candidates from all backgrounds. We believe that the more inclusive we are, the better our work will be. Please click here to find out more on our approach to Equality, Diversity and Inclusion.

So, if you are interested in this unique opportunity as a Sustainability Data Analyst, please apply via the button shown. Successful candidates will be appointed on merit.

Interviews will be held on Wednesday 1st July 2026.

Title: Claims Data Analyst
Description:
About Zego



At Zego, we understand that traditional motor insurance holds good drivers back. It's too complicated, too expensive, and it doesn't reflect how well you actually drive. Since 2016, we have been on a mission to change that by offering the lowest priced insurance for good drivers.

From van drivers and gig workers to everyday car drivers, our customers are the driving force behind everything we do. We've sold tens of millions of policies and raised over $200 million in funding and picked up numerous awards, including the Insurance Claims Team Of The Year 2024 as awarded at The Insurance Post's Claims & Fraud Awards. And we're only just getting started.

Purpose of the role



We're looking for a proactive and analytical Claims Data Analyst to help us enhance our Claims and Fraud operations, shaping one of the most vital areas of the insurance industry.

As part of a fast-growing, product-led tech company, you'll play a key role in driving impactful change within our rapidly scaling Claims team. Your insights will power strategic decision-making, improve efficiency, and uncover opportunities for continuous improvement. Expect to work on developing insightful reports and dashboards, collaborating closely with Claims and Fraud teams, and delivering data-backed recommendations to leadership and stakeholders across the business.

If you thrive in a fast-paced, data-centric environment and are eager to make a real difference in the evolution of claims operations, we'd love to hear from you!

What you will be doing


Design & build core data models and reporting to support Claims and Fraud operations
Develop key reports for senior stakeholders, including Zego leadership, investors, and insurance partners
Analyze performance & identify trends, uncovering key drivers to optimise processes
Spot and size opportunities for continuous improvement, tracking the impact of initiatives
Support strategic decision-making for the Claims and Fraud leadership team
Translate data into compelling stories—delivering actionable insights to senior management
Collaborate cross-functionally, supporting Claims, Fraud, Pricing, and external reporting needs
What you will need to be successful


Got a degree? Great! If not, equivalent experience works too (bonus points if it's in a STEM subject)
SQL Skills - You know your way around data extraction and manipulation like a pro
Power BI Expertise - You've built data models, reports, and dashboards before and can turn raw numbers into something meaningful
Data Storytelling - You can take complex insights and turn them into clear, engaging stories that senior stakeholders (think senior managers, investors, Zego's board, and insurance partners) actually understand
Insurance Know-How (Nice to have!) - If you understand the motor insurance market and what drives commercial performance, that's a big plus
Claims & Financial Acumen (Nice to have!) - Familiarity with claims processes, reserving, and settlements? Even better
Actuarial & Reserving Knowledge (Nice to have!) - If you can be the go-to person between Claims, Fraud, and Actuarial teams, we'd love to have you on board!
What's it like to work at Zego?



Joining Zego is a career-defining move. People go further here, reaching their full potential to achieve extraordinary things. 

We're spread throughout the UK and Europe, and united by our drive to get things done. We're proud of our company and our culture - a friendly and inclusive space where we can lift each other up and celebrate our wins every day.

Together, we're setting the bar higher, delivering exceptional work that makes a difference. Our people are the most important part of our story, and everyone here plays a role. There's loads of room to learn and grow, and you'll get the freedom to steer your career wherever you want.

You'll work alongside a talented group who embrace each other's differences and aren't afraid of a challenge. We recognise our achievements, learn from our mistakes, and help each other to be the best we can be. Together, we're making insurance matter. 

Our approach to AI



We believe in the power of AI to meaningfully improve how we work - helping us move faster, think differently, and focus on what matters most. At Zego, we encourage people to stay curious and intentional about how AI is leveraged in their work and teams to drive practical impact every day. This is your chance to do the most meaningful work of your career - and we'll provide you with the tools, support, and freedom to do it well.

How we work



We believe that teams work better when they have time to collaborate and space to get things done. We call it Zego Hybrid.

Our hybrid way of working is unique. We foster a flexible approach that empowers every Zegon to perform at their best. We ask you to spend at least one day a week in our central London office (next to Liverpool Street). We cover the costs for all company-wide events (3 per year), and also provide a separate hybrid contribution to help pay towards other travel costs. We think it's a good mix of collaborative face time and flexible home-working, setting us up to achieve the right balance between work and life.

Benefits



We reward our people well. Join us and you'll get a market-competitive salary, private medical insurance, company share options, generous holiday allowance, and a whole lot of wellbeing benefits. And that's just for starters.

  • Got a degree? Great! If not, equivalent experience works too (bonus points if it's in a STEM subject)
  • SQL Skills - You know your way around data extraction and manipulation like a pro
  • Power BI Expertise - You've built data models, reports, and dashboards before and can turn raw numbers into something meaningful
  • Data Storytelling - You can take complex insights and turn them into clear, engaging stories that senior stakeholders (think senior managers, investors, Zego's board, and insurance partners) actually understand
  • Insurance Know-How (Nice to have!) - If you understand the motor insurance market and what drives commercial performance, that's a big plus
  • Claims & Financial Acumen (Nice to have!) - Familiarity with claims processes, reserving, and settlements? Even better
  • Actuarial & Reserving Knowledge (Nice to have!) - If you can be the go-to person between Claims, Fraud, and Actuarial teams, we'd love to have you on board!
What's it like to work at Zego?

Joining Zego is a career-defining move. People go further here, reaching their full potential to achieve extraordinary things. 

We're spread throughout the UK and Europe, and united by our drive to get things done. We're proud of our company and our culture - a friendly and inclusive space where we can lift each other up and celebrate our wins every day.

Together, we're setting the bar higher, delivering exceptional work that makes a difference. Our people are the most important part of our story, and everyone here plays a role. There's loads of room to learn and grow, and you'll get the freedom to steer your career wherever you want.

You'll work alongside a talented group who embrace each other's differences and aren't afraid of a challenge. We recognise our achievements, learn from our mistakes, and help each other to be the best we can be. Together, we're making insurance matter. 

Our approach to AI

We believe in the power of AI to meaningfully improve how we work - helping us move faster, think differently, and focus on what matters most. At Zego, we encourage people to stay curious and intentional about how AI is leveraged in their work and teams to drive practical impact every day. This is your chance to do the most meaningful work of your career - and we'll provide you with the tools, support, and freedom to do it well.

How we work

We believe that teams work better when they have time to collaborate and space to get things done. We call it Zego Hybrid.

Our hybrid way of working is unique. We foster a flexible approach that empowers every Zegon to perform at their best. We ask you to spend at least one day a week in our central London office (next to Liverpool Street). We cover the costs for all company-wide events (3 per year), and also provide a separate hybrid contribution to help pay towards other travel costs. We think it's a good mix of collaborative face time and flexible home-working, setting us up to achieve the right balance between work and life.

Benefits

We reward our people well. Join us and you'll get a market-competitive salary, private medical insurance, company share options, generous holiday allowance, and a whole lot of wellbeing benefits. And that's just for starters.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: City Of London, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Data Analyst – London




5-6 Month Contract




Up to £525 per day | Outside IR35




Hybrid Working – 1-2 Days Per Week Onsite




We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join an exciting transformation programme focused on evolving how the organisation uses data to drive better business decisions.

This role will play a key part in enabling self-service analytics by leveraging Looker to create semantic layers across core datasets. These semantic models will support Looker AI Agents (Looker Conversation), Large Language Models (LLMs) and automated push notifications, giving business users greater flexibility to explore data, receive proactive insights and accelerate decision-making.

A significant focus of this role will be working with LLM-powered AI solutions, reviewing large and complex datasets, validating AI-generated outputs and ensuring that insights delivered to the business are accurate, reliable and fit for purpose. You'll be responsible for maintaining rigorous quality assurance standards and will have the confidence to sign off AI-generated outputs before they are released to the business.

Working closely with business stakeholders, data engineering teams and analytics specialists, you'll help shape how AI, semantic modelling and trusted data are used across the organisation.




Key skills and responsibilities,

Looker expertise – Proven hands-on experience developing and working within the Looker platform, including semantic layer creation and optimisation.
Looker AI Agents – Experience working with or supporting AI Agents within Looker (Looker Conversation), helping to optimise semantic models and improve AI-generated responses.
Automated Push Notifications – Experience building or supporting automated push notifications and proactive insight delivery using data-driven workflows.
LLM & AI experience – Strong experience working with Large Language Models (LLMs), AI-powered analytics or conversational AI solutions, ensuring outputs are accurate, relevant and trusted.
Large Dataset Analysis – Extensive experience analysing, interrogating and validating large, complex datasets, identifying anomalies and ensuring data integrity across AI-driven solutions.
Semantic Layer Development – Collaborate with business domain experts to design, build and optimise semantic layers, working closely with Data Engineering to improve performance and scalability.
Quality Assurance – Significant experience testing, validating and quality assuring data products and AI-generated outputs, with exceptional attention to detail and a critical eye for accuracy.
Results Ownership – Confident taking accountability for data quality and formally signing off whether AI-generated insights meet business and delivery standards.
AI & Machine Learning Lifecycle – Previous experience supporting AI/ML initiatives and AI agents from development through to deployment, with an understanding of model validation and continuous improvement.
SQL Proficiency – Strong working knowledge of SQL for analysing, interrogating and validating large datasets.
Stakeholder Engagement – Partner with business stakeholders to understand key business questions and translate requirements into trusted analytical models.
Adaptability – Comfortable working in an agile environment where priorities evolve, AI capabilities mature and continuous learning is encouraged.




This is an exciting opportunity to work at the forefront of AI-enabled analytics, helping shape how the organisation leverages LLMs, Looker AI Agents, semantic modelling and automated push notifications to transform how data is consumed and trusted across the business.




Interested? Please submit your updated CV to Dean Sadler-Parkes at Harvey Nash for immediate consideration.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst - London - Up to £50K



Hybrid working - 3 days a week onsite

SQL | Power BI | Snowflake

Are you a Data Analyst with strong SQL and Power BI skills looking to make a real impact?

We're partnering with an organisation that's investing heavily in its data capabilities and is looking for a talented Data Analyst to play a key role in transforming data into meaningful business insight.

This isn't just another reporting role. We're looking for someone with excellent data fundamentals-someone who understands how data is structured, modelled and transformed before bringing it to life through intuitive Power BI dashboards. You'll work across the full dashboard development lifecycle, partnering with stakeholders to turn complex business requirements into impactful reporting solutions that drive better decision-making.



The Role



You'll be responsible for:

Designing and developing engaging Power BI dashboards from concept through to deployment.
Gathering requirements, creating wireframes and delivering reporting that answers real business questions.
Writing and optimising SQL queries to extract, manipulate and prepare data.
Analysing complex datasets to identify trends, opportunities and actionable insights.
Working alongside Data Engineers to improve data pipelines and support scalable data models.
Ensuring data quality, accuracy and governance across reporting solutions.
Building strong relationships with stakeholders and acting as a trusted partner for data and analytics.
We're keen to speak with Data Analysts who have a solid understanding of core data principles and enjoy solving business problems through data.

Essential skills include:
Strong SQL skills.
Proven experience building dashboards in Power BI.
Excellent understanding of data fundamentals, including data modelling, data preparation and data quality.
Experience translating business requirements into reporting and dashboard solutions.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication skills with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Nice to have (but not essential):

Snowflake experience.
Python or R.
Microsoft Azure.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At Smart, our mission is to transform retirement, savings and financial wellbeing, across all generations, around the world.

THE ROLE

As part of the Data Analytics team, you will work closely with stakeholders to translate business challenges into data problems, delivering high-quality and clearly articulated analysis, reporting, and data-driven insights that support effective decision-making. We are a small team with big ambitions. We are building the foundations to enable our vision of maximising the value that our extensive dataset and modern data stack can generate for the business. Because we support the entire business, our data is varied and complex. As such, we are seeking a self-starter who is comfortable navigating ambiguity and independently finding solutions within a fast paced environment. If you match our ambitions, are passionate about creating value from data, and want to help drive the evolution of our data capabilities, then we would love to hear from you.

What You Will Do

Stakeholder Partnership & Communication


Define requirements: Partner with stakeholders across the company to translate business questions into clear analytics requirements and identify the right data points.
Data Translation & Storytelling: Explain analytical outputs and insights to stakeholders in clear, non-technical terms and, as required, build a narrative to support effective decision-making.
Drive data literacy: Support initiatives that enable stakeholders to self-serve data more effectively.


Analytics & Business Intelligence


Build BI solutions: Design, build, and maintain reports and dashboards (primarily using Tableau) used across the organisation, including Board-level KPI reporting.
Data Analysis & Insights: Manipulate and analyse complex data to answer business questions and identify trends, insights, and opportunities.


Data Modeling & Quality Assurance


Build robust data models: Develop and maintain optimised and reliable solutions in our data warehouse (Redshift) using tools like dbt, collaborating closely with Data Engineering where required.
Ensure trust & accuracy: Maintain data quality and reporting reliability, manage the impact of upstream changes, and support business-critical processes with direct regulatory or customer impact.
Peer review: Participate in code reviews and collaborative QA to validate the accuracy and value of our analytical output.


Continuous Improvement & Team Culture


Automate & optimise: Identify opportunities to improve efficiency through automation, better data processes and the responsible use of AI.
Be an active team player: Contribute your ideas to team discussions, retrospectives, and future planning to continuously improve how we work together and support the business.


Who We Are Looking For

The skills, experience, and aptitudes we are looking for are listed below but please don’t be discouraged from applying if you don’t meet every single one of these criteria – having a ‘can do’ attitude is sometimes more important than being able to tick every box:

Experience & Attributes


Proven track record: 3+ years of experience in data analysis, with a focus on data manipulation, reporting and insight analytics.
Stakeholder partnership: A proven ability to work with stakeholders to deeply understand business problems, translate requirements into effective analytics solutions, and advise on best approaches.
Data storytelling & coaching: An effective technical communicator who can translate complex data for non-technical audiences and actively coach stakeholders to enhance overall data maturity.
Analytical curiosity: Inquisitive by nature, with sharp attention to detail and a natural drive to look beyond the surface numbers to uncover actionable insights.
Ambitious and agile learner: A fast, curious learner who is eager to independently explore and pick up new datasets, technologies and methodologies to innovate with purpose.
Organised & adaptable: Highly organised with the ability to manage multiple concurrent tasks.
Collaborative team player: A reliable team player with a "team-first" mindset who thrives in a highly collaborative and supportive environment.
Financial Services/Pensions experience (Desirable): Experience within financial services is desirable; exposure to the pensions industry is a plus.


Technical Skills


Advanced SQL (Essential): Extensive experience of writing, troubleshooting, and optimising complex queries.
BI & Data Visualisation (Essential - Tableau Preferred): Strong experience building impactful dashboards and reports in a modern BI tool, with a preference for Tableau.
dbt (Desirable): Experience with dbt for data modeling, transformation, and managing the analytics layer.
Python (Desirable): Experience using Python for data manipulation, advanced analytics or automation.
Git / GitHub (Desirable): experience using version control for managing code, collaborating with others, and reviewing changes.


Who We Are

We work in partnerships with governments and financial institutions in the UK and internationally. Our cloud-native digital platform is revolutionising how people around the world think about, and save for, their retirement.

At heart, we’re a financial technology business. What we do is all about innovation, and using the power of digital change to put the customer first. Our Engineers will tell you that working at Smart gives you the opportunity to play your part in developing world-class technological solutions, working with – and learning from – like-minded people.

You’ll also find that, across our business, our colleagues love Smart’s culture, and how what we do means better financial outcomes for savers. That feels worthwhile, and it means that what we do, collectively, goes way beyond the nine to five of a typical working day.

Don’t just take our word for it – you can see what our colleagues say about working at Smart on LinkedIn Life and Glassdoor.

Benefits

At Smart, one of the eight principles we work to is “We want happy and good people in our team”. We created a list of benefits that helps us achieve this goal:


25 days’ holiday per year, increasing with length of service.
£500 annual training budget to spend on your professional development.
Extensive private healthcare, including dental, eyecare and EAP.
Enhanced sick leave (three months’ pay per year).
Enhanced maternity and paternity (maternity – 6 months fully paid/paternity – 3 weeks fully paid).
Death in service insurance cover.
Fully-paid five-week sabbatical after five years of employment.
In office wellbeing, such as manicures, massages and barbers.
Smart employees also enjoy a 50% discount on orders from our sister company Arena Flowers, Britain's most ethical florist. They offer unique hand-tied bouquets, luxury flowers, letterbox flowers, plants and gifts to spend on friends and loved ones or even for yourself.


At Smart, we are committed to creating an inclusive and equitable workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to do their best work.

We believe that diverse perspectives help us lead the way in transforming retirement, savings, and financial wellbeing. We welcome differences in background, experience, thinking, and identity, and we recognise that innovation is strongest when it is built on inclusion and fairness. We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds and experiences and do not discriminate on the basis of any protected characteristic.

If you require any reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process or in the workplace, we encourage you to let us know - we are committed to supporting you.

We think Smart is an awesome place to work. If it sounds like somewhere you’d like to work, too, and if you’re ready to play your part in our continued success in the future, then naturally we’d love to meet you.

Our mission is to transform retirement, savings and financial wellbeing, across all generations, around the world.

Title: Data Analyst, Global Viewership
Description:
Workplace: White City, London - 3 days a week in the office




The role




As part of the global data and insights team at ITV Studios, our goal is to deliver the best global intelligence from brilliant data sets to maximise revenue across Global Sales for scripted, unscripted and formats, Zoo55, Brand Partnerships via Studio 55 as well for Content & Production.




As the data analyst within the Global Viewing team, you will be responsible for delivering the insights which support our global content strategy and sales. While viewing data is our core currency, you go further, triangulating Ratings (BARB/Nielsen) with Market data Intelligence (Ampere, K7, NoTa) and Social Sentiment (i.e. Sprinklr) to build a 360-degree view of success. You focus on strategic, forward-looking analysis that helps us understand why content travels, puts performance into context for the channel, platform or market, and consider the business and commercial implications of your findings.




The role




Data Alignment: Support the senior and lead analyst defining the technical data mapping across territories. You ensure that our key content brands (e.g. Love Island) are accurately aligned across international datasets to maintain a "Single Source of Truth" for our key content brands
Data Extraction: Use SQL and viewing platforms (e.g., Techedge/BARB) to extract performance data. You handle regular and ad-hoc data retrieval for the sales teams, moving beyond standard dashboards to find specific answers.
Data Integrity: Maintain the day-to-day quality of our data.
Market Data Augmentation: Assist in the collection of alternative metrics for "data-poor" markets. You help find and organize non-traditional data to provide a clearer picture where official ratings are limited.




Insight delivery




Territory Analysis: Support in developing a good understanding of viewership trends in specific international markets. You provide the "so what" by putting show performances into the context of local platform trends.
Performance Tracking: Take ownership of monitoring and tracking the international performance of our titles, providing clear summaries and identifying standout hits for the business.
Sales Support: Translate complex data into persuasive "sales stories." You provide the evidence-based insights that help our teams pitch and sell shows to global buyers.
Forecasting Support: Update and maintain the benchmarking models used to predict show success. You ensure comparison sets are relevant and up-to-date for new launches.
Contextual Intelligence: augment TV ratings reporting by integrating market trends and social data intelligence




Dashboarding & automation




Dashboard Optimization: Update and enhance interactive visualization dashboards (Looker/Tableau). You help stakeholders transition from manual Excel files to self-serve automated tools by ensuring views are accurate and user-friendly.
Automation Identification: Proactively identify repetitive manual tasks in your workflow. You work closely with the Senior Analyst to define requirements for technical data pipelining, helping to move the team toward more automated solutions.




Skills you’ll need (minimum criteria)




Demonstrable experience in a data analytics or business intelligence role, with a proven track record of using SQL to query and manipulate datasets. You must be comfortable working with raw data and have an interest in data quality and enrichment.
Working proficiency with cloud-based platforms such as GCP (BigQuery) or equivalent to extract and process data.

Experience translating data into clear, actionable insights for commercial or sales teams. You can go beyond "what the number is" to explain "what the number means" for a show or market.
Ability to maintain and enhance interactive dashboards using Tableau, Looker, or PowerBI. You are comfortable helping stakeholders transition from static reports to self-serve tools.
Proven track record of using data to drive business value in a global content sales business

Comfortable presenting findings across a range of formats, ensuring the "so what" is clear whether in a slide, a dashboard, or a brief email.

HTML:
Workplace: White City, London - 3 days a week in the office


The role


As part of the global data and insights team at ITV Studios, our goal is to deliver the best global intelligence from brilliant data sets to maximise revenue across Global Sales for scripted, unscripted and formats, Zoo55, Brand Partnerships via Studio 55 as well for Content & Production.


As the data analyst within the Global Viewing team, you will be responsible for delivering the insights which support our global content strategy and sales. While viewing data is our core currency, you go further, triangulating Ratings (BARB/Nielsen) with Market data Intelligence (Ampere, K7, NoTa) and Social Sentiment (i.e. Sprinklr) to build a 360-degree view of success. You focus on strategic, forward-looking analysis that helps us understand why content travels, puts performance into context for the channel, platform or market, and consider the business and commercial implications of your findings.


The role


  • Data Alignment: Support the senior and lead analyst defining the technical data mapping across territories. You ensure that our key content brands (e.g. Love Island) are accurately aligned across international datasets to maintain a "Single Source of Truth" for our key content brands
  • Data Extraction: Use SQL and viewing platforms (e.g., Techedge/BARB) to extract performance data. You handle regular and ad-hoc data retrieval for the sales teams, moving beyond standard dashboards to find specific answers.
  • Data Integrity: Maintain the day-to-day quality of our data.
  • Market Data Augmentation: Assist in the collection of alternative metrics for "data-poor" markets. You help find and organize non-traditional data to provide a clearer picture where official ratings are limited.


Insight delivery


  • Territory Analysis: Support in developing a good understanding of viewership trends in specific international markets. You provide the "so what" by putting show performances into the context of local platform trends.
  • Performance Tracking: Take ownership of monitoring and tracking the international performance of our titles, providing clear summaries and identifying standout hits for the business.
  • Sales Support: Translate complex data into persuasive "sales stories." You provide the evidence-based insights that help our teams pitch and sell shows to global buyers.
  • Forecasting Support: Update and maintain the benchmarking models used to predict show success. You ensure comparison sets are relevant and up-to-date for new launches.
  • Contextual Intelligence: augment TV ratings reporting by integrating market trends and social data intelligence


Dashboarding & automation


  • Dashboard Optimization: Update and enhance interactive visualization dashboards (Looker/Tableau). You help stakeholders transition from manual Excel files to self-serve automated tools by ensuring views are accurate and user-friendly.
  • Automation Identification: Proactively identify repetitive manual tasks in your workflow. You work closely with the Senior Analyst to define requirements for technical data pipelining, helping to move the team toward more automated solutions.


Skills you’ll need (minimum criteria)


  • Demonstrable experience in a data analytics or business intelligence role, with a proven track record of using SQL to query and manipulate datasets. You must be comfortable working with raw data and have an interest in data quality and enrichment.
  • Working proficiency with cloud-based platforms such as GCP (BigQuery) or equivalent to extract and process data.
  • Sound knowledge of international viewing currencies and software (e.g., Techedge, Nielsen, BARB). You understand the core metrics of linear (ratings, share) and the basic principles of streaming data.
  • Experience translating data into clear, actionable insights for commercial or sales teams. You can go beyond "what the number is" to explain "what the number means" for a show or market.
  • Ability to maintain and enhance interactive dashboards using Tableau, Looker, or PowerBI. You are comfortable helping stakeholders transition from static reports to self-serve tools.
  • Proven track record of using data to drive business value in a global content sales business
  • Able to identify and question potential biases or anomalies in analytical output and datasets rather than taking them at face value.
  • Comfortable presenting findings across a range of formats, ensuring the "so what" is clear whether in a slide, a dashboard, or a brief email.
  • You have a good understanding of the global TV and streaming landscape and a desire to understand the drivers behind content success and can lean on that knowledge to provide qualitative creative conclusions alongside hard data

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst / Senior Data Analyst, Marketing Analytics
Southwark, London. £40,000 - £60,000 + benefits. Hybrid working, 3 days in office.
This is an excellent opportunity to join a growing marketing analytics function within a leading customer-focused organisation. You will work across a variety of clients and projects, combining customer insight with commercial impact, while developing a well-rounded skillset in a collaborative, fast-paced environment.
The Company
They are an established customer experience and marketing analytics organisation that partners with well-known brands to improve how they engage with their customers. Data sits at the core of their offering, with a strong focus on analytics, data science, and measurement. The business operates within a wider network, providing exposure to large-scale marketing activity and cross-functional collaboration. They offer a flexible structure that supports internal mobility and career development across different client accounts.
The Role
You will:

Deliver analytics across campaign performance, marketing effectiveness, and customer journeys
Analyse customer behaviour to generate actionable insights that balance experience and commercial outcomes
Build, maintain, and improve dashboards and reports using modern visualisation tools
Work with analytics platforms to answer client questions and provide insight-driven recommendations
Collaborate with technical, data, and strategy teams to ensure accurate data capture and tracking
Support and contribute to end-to-end project delivery in an agile environment
At senior level, you will also:

Take ownership of project delivery and outputs
Support the direction of analytics strategy across accounts
Work closely with stakeholders and build strong client relationships
Guide and mentor junior analysts
Your Skills & Experience
You will have:

Strong commercial experience with SQL and or Python
Hands-on experience using analytics tools such as GA4, Adobe Analytics, or similar
Experience delivering data visualisation and communicating insights clearly
Strong understanding of marketing analytics, including campaign performance and customer journey analysis
Ability to translate data into business value and actionable recommendations
Experience working in a fast-paced, client-focused or agency environment
For more senior roles:

Confident stakeholder management and client-facing experience
Ability to support or mentor other analysts
Experience contributing to strategic analytics initiatives
What They Offer

Competitive salary of £40,000 - £60,000 depending on experience
Hybrid working with a central London office
Clear progression opportunities with structured mobility across teams
Exposure to a wide range of clients and marketing challenges
A collaborative and supportive team environment focused on continuous learning
How to Apply
Apply now with your CV to be considered for this opportunity.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
The Principal responsibilities will involve:




Turn large quantities of complex client data into insights which can be used to inform decision making and drive business benefit
Are quick to identify incomplete and diverse data sets, pin pointing data issues which may affect the accuracy and completeness of any analysis performed
Recognise the importance and value of understanding the data they are analysing, validating the information provided and reconciling the data sets as part of the Data Analytics Cycle
Produce data visualisations, detailed reporting and analytics underpinned with clear business commentary
Work closely with multi-disciplined teams to deliver end to end client solutions in a timely manner
Balance the technical and analytical demands of their role
Are open minded and flexible with a thirst for knowledge and an appetite for continuous development




Responsibilities




Clear, concise and insightful data analytics which enable our clients to make sound business decisions based on fact
Ability to translate data analysis into targeted information which can be converted into actionable improvements, based on specific client / industry need
Continued improvement of Sagacity’s Product Suite through the delivery of robust data insight
Ability to take accountability and ownership for client and internal deliverables
Work as part of a Data Analytics team proving knowledge transfer support, peer to peer reviews and mentoring as a team to increase the team’s skill sites and drive continuous learning




Qualifications




Proficiency in analytical programming language such as python and/or SQL
Understanding of relational databases and concepts for querying data




Required Skills




Proficiency in tools like SQL, Tableau, and/or Power BI
Can balance time across multiple projects. Plans ahead working backwards from deadlines with all necessary steps e.g. testing, QA. Proactively identifies risk and suggests mitigations
Is inquisitive, suggests ‘next steps’ analysis and translates analytical findings to actionable insight
Commercial experience within Telecoms, Banking or Utilities industries; or within a data related consultancy company would be beneficial
Able to travel throughout the UK
Can be based at our London Office (min 2 days per week on site)




Preferred Skills




Experience with data visualization tools and techniques
Strong communication skills for presenting findings to stakeholders

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst (Freelance, Outside IR35, 1 month initial ) — Fashion Retail

London (Hybrid, mainly remote)




We're supporting a PE-backed fashion retail client on a freelance basis to help bring clarity to their inventory data.




The client's inventory data currently sits across around ten different spreadsheets with no single agreed source of truth. We're looking for a sharp, personable Data Analyst to get under the hood, work directly with the client's team, and build a clean, centralised baseline of their current inventory position.




What you'll be doing:




Working closely with client stakeholders to identify the source of truth across multiple spreadsheets
Asking the right questions to untangle inconsistent, messy data
Using Excel (and ideally AI tools) to build a clear, centralised view of inventory status
Bringing structure and clarity — this is not a data engineering or system-building role




What we're looking for:




Strong Excel skills -non-negotiable
Comfortable using AI tools to speed up data wrangling
Confident, personable, and comfortable working with non-technical, client-side stakeholders
Patient, low-ego, and happy to dig through messy spreadsheets to get to the answer
Fashion retail experience is a bonus, not a must




The details:




Freelance engagement, outside IR35
London-based, hybrid working — mainly remote
Single-stage interview process — moving quickly, with slots available this week

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
🚀 Data Analyst / Data Modeler – Contract – Outside IR35

📍 Fully Remote (UK-based)

💰 £550–£600 per day

📅 Initial 3 months (high likelihood of 6+ months)

🚀 Start: ASAP (early July)

🐾 Exciting Opportunity in Data, Knowledge Graphs & Veterinary Tech




I’m working with a leading pet insurance & veterinary services organisation on a high-profile data initiative and they’re looking for a hands-on Data Analyst / Modeler to help shape the future of their UK and European data strategy.




This is a rare chance to work with a pre-built US veterinary ontology (covering conditions, treatments, and pharmaceuticals) and assess how it can be adapted for the UK market.




You’ll play a key role in determining whether this model can become the foundation of a long-term, strategic data solution.




🔍 What You’ll Be Doing

Deep-diving into a complex graph-based veterinary dataset
Analysing and validating how a US ontology maps to UK requirements
Identifying gaps, inconsistencies, and localisation needs
Acting as the bridge between technical teams and veterinary experts (UK & US)
Performing semantic/search-based analysis across graph & relational data
Supporting data modelling decisions that will shape the future architecture




👉 Expect a split of roughly 70% analysis / 30% modelling




✅ Must-Have Experience

Strong, hands-on data analysis skills
Experience with Neo4j / graph databases
Solid understanding of ontologies, taxonomies, and data modelling
Strong SQL
Excellent communication — able to translate between technical & clinical audiences
Comfort diving into unfamiliar domains (medical/veterinary concepts)




⭐ Nice to Have

Cypher / GraphQL or similar
Experience with knowledge graphs or semantic models
Exposure to medical, pharmaceutical, or insurance data
Comfortable working in ambiguous, fast-moving environments




💡 Who This Suits




Someone pragmatic, curious, and proactive happy to:

Get stuck into data
Facilitate conversations with experts
Help define the solution as they go




If you enjoy high-impact work, autonomy, and solving complex data problems, this role is for you.




📩 Interested? Apply now or message me directly to learn more.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Senior Data Analyst / Analytics Engineer

Location: UK-Based | Hybrid Working (3 Days in Office per Week)




We are seeking an experienced Senior Data Analyst / Analytics Engineer to join our growing team. This is an exciting opportunity for a data professional who combines strong technical expertise with exceptional stakeholder management skills and a passion for turning complex data into meaningful business insights.




As a senior member of the analytics function, you will lead the development of data visualisations, reporting frameworks, and analytics solutions that drive strategic decision-making across the business.




Key Responsibilities:




Lead the design, development, and delivery of interactive dashboards and data visualisations using Power BI, Excel, SQL, Tableau, and/or Power Automate.
Collaborate with stakeholders across the organisation to gather requirements and translate complex business challenges into scalable analytics and reporting solutions.
Take ownership of data quality processes, including data cleansing, transformation, validation, and the development of reliable reporting models.
Define and implement best practices for data modelling, reporting standards, and visualisation design.
Present insights, trends, and actionable recommendations to senior leadership and cross-functional teams in a clear and compelling manner.
Partner with engineering, product, and operational teams to automate data processes, optimise reporting infrastructure, and enhance existing analytics frameworks.
Act as a technical leader within the team, promoting a culture of innovation, collaboration, continuous improvement, and analytical excellence.
Support and mentor team members while contributing as a hands-on analytics specialist.




Skills & Experience:




Experience in Data Analytics, Analytics Engineering, Power BI Development, or a closely related field.
Advanced proficiency in Power BI, SQL, and Excel, with experience creating high-quality dashboards and reporting solutions.
Experience using Tableau and/or Power Automate.
Proven track record of gathering requirements, managing stakeholders, and translating business needs into analytical solutions.
Strong expertise in data transformation, cleansing, modelling, and preparing datasets for scalable reporting and analytics.
Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to engage both technical and non-technical audiences.
Experience developing user-focused, visually engaging dashboards and data products.
Strong problem-solving skills and a continuous improvement mindset.




Desirable

Power BI and/or Microsoft Azure certifications.
Experience using Python for analytics or automation.
Previous experience in a Business Analyst capacity.




What's Required

Must be based in the UK and able to commute to the office three days per week.
Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced environment while managing multiple priorities.
Passion for data, analytics, and delivering measurable business value through insights.




If you're an experienced analytics professional looking to take ownership of impactful data initiatives and help shape a data-driven culture, we'd love to hear from you.

Title: Data Research Analyst – Financial Services (Remote)
Description:
At Forbes Advisor, our mission is to help readers turn their aspirations into reality. We arm people with trusted advice and guidance so they can make informed decisions they feel confident in and get back to doing the things they care about most.



We are an experienced team of industry experts dedicated to helping readers make smart decisions and choose the right products with ease. Forbes Advisor boasts decades of experience across dozens of geographies and teams, including Content, SEO, Business Intelligence, Finance, HR, Marketing, Production, Technology and Sales. The team brings rich industry knowledge to Forbes Advisor's global coverage of consumer credit, debt, health, home improvement, banking, investing, credit cards, small business, education, insurance, loans, real estate and travel.





Job Description



We are seeking a Data Research Analyst to join our team and assist our editorial team in creating top-quality content. In this role, you will provide competitive analysis on the market and ensure the quality and accuracy of data collection and analysis for finicial products and services relevant. Your research and analysis will be a key component of making trustworthy, relevant assessments of products and services, and will help thousands of businesses make informed decisions about the products and services they use.



You will also be responsible for assigning projects to researchers. You will be liaising with the data research team leads, editorial, methodology, and SEO teams to address challenges, fulfill data requests, and deliver analysis used to produce editorial content on the Forbes Advisor websites. Leveraging project management and data skills, you will help the Finserv division deliver actionable insights on time, with an eye to grow the capability and capacity of the Finserv research team.



Responsibilities:



Review data requests for feasibility, usability, and scalability
Leverage a team of researchers to collect product and service data
Perform analysis on collected data to identify top-quality products and providers
Build analyses on Tableau against tight deadlines whilst ensuring accuracy and quality
Develop and implement processes for quality control, performing checks on data gathered by the research team and actioning identified errors
Communicate with the data research team leads and editorial teams to maintain transparency about the research process, stages of analysis, and any hurdles to meeting delivery deadlines
Review editorial content resulting from work product for accuracy and completeness
Scope for future line management of the analyst(s), monitoring performance and providing support for them to succeed and develop their career

About You:



Able to leverage project management skills to ensure consistent and on-time delivery across a range of projects and teams with differing and at times conflicting priorities and deadlines
Takes pride in developing specialist product knowledge, becoming a main point of contact and source of information, while keeping business needs in mind
Experience with US Financial Services markets, particularly lending products.
Comfortable working in SQL and Microsoft Excel
Have previously communicated data findings to project stakeholders via dashboards and data visualisations, using software such as Tableau or PowerBI
You are mindful of maintaining a supportive working environment conducive to helping colleagues and direct reports understand the current state of projects, keeping communication channels clear and open

Qualifications:



Degree in a relevant field, such as business or economics, or equivalent training and experience
Proficiency in using Tableau and spreadsheets for data analysis and manipulation
Proficiency in SQL and Python for data manipulation and visualisation, is highly desirable
Experience in working as part of an international team across time zones is desirable

Benefits:



Competitive salary
Medical, dental and vision coverage
35 Days of PTO (Including Bank Holidays)
Every third Friday of the month off
Disability coverage
Employee assistance program
Paid parental leave program


Additional Information



Forbes Advisor provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.



This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation and training.
