Title: Chief Technology Officer
Description:
Chief Technology Officer (CTO)

Location: Toronto, Ontario (Hybrid) or Montréal, Québec (Hybrid)

Reports to: CEO

Level: Executive / C-Suite

About us:

Trolley is the payouts platform for the internet economy. Our mission is to unlock the global economic opportunity of the internet by building a truly borderless payouts ecosystem.

Through our platform and API, Trolley enables businesses to pay people anywhere in the world, supporting creators, on demand workers, and suppliers at scale. Companies rely on Trolley as mission-critical infrastructure to automate payouts, collect tax and banking information, and reduce fraud and risk, all in one place.

Today, Trolley is the trusted payouts solution for hundreds of businesses, including Canva, Soundcloud, and Envato. We’ve processed millions of payments globally, helping creators, artists, and suppliers get paid quickly, securely, and with confidence.

As Trolley enters its next stage of growth, we will be heavily investing in AI to accelerate product innovation, improve operational efficiency, and transform how software is built across the company. We are seeking a Chief Technology Officer (CTO) to lead our technology organization and help define how AI shapes the future of Trolley.

The Opportunity

The CTO will be a key member of Trolley's executive leadership team, responsible for defining and executing the company's technology strategy while ensuring the scalability, security, reliability, and innovation of our platform.

A critical mandate for the successful candidate will be driving AI adoption across the engineering team and broader company. We are looking for a leader who has successfully implemented AI powered development practices, enabled organization wide adoption, and delivered measurable improvements in engineering productivity, software quality, and speed of execution.

The CTO will oversee Engineering and Information Security while partnering closely with Product, Operations, Finance, Compliance, and GTM leaders to align technology investments with company objectives.

What You’ll Do

Define and Execute Trolley's Technology Strategy


Define and execute Trolley's long term technology vision and roadmap.
Partner with the CEO and executive leadership team to align technology investments with company strategy and growth objectives.
Ensure the platform remains scalable, secure, reliable, and capable of supporting Trolley's continued global expansion.
Evaluate emerging technologies and identify opportunities to create competitive advantage and improve operational efficiency.
Lead architectural decisions that support long term product innovation and business growth.


Lead AI Transformation Across the Organization


Define and execute Trolley's AI strategy across Engineering and the broader organization.
Drive adoption of AI powered software development practices, coding assistants, testing automation, QA automation, observability, and operational tooling.
Establish measurable improvements in engineering productivity, software quality, and delivery velocity through AI.
Identify opportunities to leverage AI within products, customer experiences, internal operations, and decision making processes.
Build a culture of continuous learning and responsible AI adoption.
Serve as Trolley's executive leader for AI innovation.


Lead and Scale Engineering


Build, develop, and lead a high performing engineering team.
Establish a culture of accountability, ownership, collaboration, and technical excellence.
Recruit, mentor, and retain exceptional engineering leaders and talent.
Implement scalable engineering processes, operating rhythms, and performance metrics.
Ensure consistent delivery of secure, reliable, and high quality software.


Oversee Information Security


Lead Trolley's Information Security strategy and function, with the Head of InfoSec.
Ensure strong security, privacy, and risk management practices across the organization.
Maintain robust cybersecurity controls, incident response capabilities, and business continuity planning.
Partner with Compliance and Risk teams to support customer requirements, audits, certifications, and regulatory obligations.
Foster a security first culture while enabling innovation and speed.


Executive Leadership


Serve as a trusted advisor to the CEO and Board on technology, AI, cybersecurity, and innovation.
Contribute to company wide strategic planning and operational excellence.
Represent Trolley with customers, partners, investors, and industry stakeholders when appropriate.
Help shape the future direction of the company as a member of the executive leadership team.


What We're Looking For

Required Experience


10+ years of progressive technology leadership experience, including experience leading large engineering organizations.
Experience as a CTO, VP Technology, VP Engineering, SVP Engineering, or equivalent senior technology executive within a high-growth SaaS, fintech, payments, marketplace, or technology company.
Demonstrated success implementing AI powered engineering practices and driving meaningful adoption across software development teams.
Proven track record of delivering measurable productivity, quality, and execution improvements through AI enabled development.
Deep understanding of modern software architecture, cloud infrastructure, distributed systems, and platform scalability.
Experience leading technology organizations through periods of significant growth and organizational change.
Experience overseeing Information Security programs and cybersecurity strategy.
Strong track record of recruiting, developing, and retaining world-class technical talent.


Preferred Experience


Experience in fintech, payments, financial infrastructure, or regulated software environments.
Experience leading AI transformation initiatives beyond engineering teams.
Familiarity with modern AI development tooling, agentic workflows, automation platforms, and emerging AI technologies.
Experience scaling globally distributed platforms and engineering teams


What we offer:


Join an amazing team of diverse people working together on a common mission.
Competitive salary & annual Leadership bonus
ESOP Program: Meaningful equity and ownership that aligns your success with ours
Extended healthcare benefits from Day 1.
4 weeks paid vacation per year + public holidays (+ 1 extra vacation day per year of service).
30 days remote work from anywhere.
Gym membership allowance.
1/2 day Fridays off during summer.
Company-provided use of Apple MacBook Pro and Mac gear.

  • Strong knowledge of financial products, services, and investment solutions

Agile & Project Management

  • Demonstrated expertise in Agile and Scrum methodologies
  • Experience leading sprint planning, daily standups, and retrospectives
  • Proven ability to deliver within sprint cycles and manage scope effectively
  • Strong experience in release planning and backlog prioritization

Stakeholder Management & Collaboration

  • Excellent stakeholder engagement and management capabilities
  • Strong ability to identify and document business needs from diverse stakeholder groups
  • Experience managing conflicting priorities and driving consensus
  • Proven cross functional team collaboration with development, QA, operations, and business teams
  • ability to translate technical concepts for non technical audiences

Confluence & Documentation

  • Expert level Confluence administration and knowledge management
  • Proficiency in creating and maintaining comprehensive documentation repositories
  • Experience developing business requirements documents (BRD) and functional specifications
  • Strong capability in creating process flow diagrams, system design documentation, and user stories
  • Ability to manage traceability matrices and maintain documentation standards

Jira Administration & Workflow Management

  • Advanced Jira administration with custom workflow configuration
  • Experience managing issue tracking, sprint boards, and release management
  • Proficiency in reporting and dashboarding for project visibility
  • Strong understanding of Jira integrations with development tools

Requirements Documentation & Analysis

  • Expert level business requirements documentation (BRD) development
  • Proficiency in writing clear, detailed functional specifications
  • Strong user story creation with acceptance criteria and technical specifications
  • Experience developing and maintaining requirements traceability matrices
  • Ability to conduct requirements validation workshops

API Testing & Technical Knowledge

  • Hands on experience with API testing tools and methodologies
  • Ability to validate API functionality, response times, and error handling
  • Understanding of API documentation and integration patterns
  • Knowledge of JSON/XML data formats and REST API principles

SQL & Database Knowledge

  • Working knowledge of SQL for data queries and analysis
  • Experience retrieving and validating data from databases
  • Ability to perform basic data validation and reconciliation
  • Understanding of database relationships and data structures

Quality Assurance & User Acceptance Testing (UAT)

  • Proven UAT coordination and execution experience
  • Expert level test case development and management
  • Strong defect tracking and resolution management
  • Experience creating comprehensive UAT plans and test scenarios
  • Ability to identify, document, and prioritize defects

Demo & Presentation Skills

  • Strong presentation and demonstration abilities
  • Experience delivering product demos to stakeholders and clients
  • Ability to showcase features and gather feedback effectively
  • Proficiency in creating demo scripts and supporting materials

Additional Core Competencies

  • Analytical and problem solving skills with attention to detail
  • Change management and process improvement experience
  • Risk identification and mitigation capabilities
  • Ability to work effectively in fast paced, dynamic environments
  • Strong time management and organizational skills

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Product Analytics
Description:
Job Requisition ID #

26WD99763

Position Overview

Data is central to how we understand customer behavior, measure product success, and build better experiences for our customers. Autodesk is seeking a Senior Data Analyst - Product Analytics to join the Product Development & Manufacturing Solutions organization. In this role, you'll partner closely with Product Managers, Engineering, Design, Finance, Sales, and Marketing to transform product data into actionable insights that influence product strategy and business outcomes.

As a trusted analytics partner, you will help answer critical questions about feature adoption, customer engagement, retention, and product health. You'll define meaningful success metrics, develop scalable measurement frameworks, and uncover opportunities to improve the customer experience through data.

We're looking for someone who thrives in ambiguity, is naturally curious, and enjoys asking difficult questions that lead to better decisions. You combine strong analytical thinking with business acumen and exceptional communication skills to translate complex analyses into compelling stories that drive action.

At Autodesk, we value a hybrid work culture. Remote work is supported in Toronto and Vancouver, Canada. If you live near one of our offices, you're welcome to work there full- or part-time based on your preference.

Responsibilities


Partner with Product Managers and Engineering teams throughout the product lifecycle to define success metrics and measurement strategies before features launch
Analyze product telemetry, clickstream, and user behavior data to identify opportunities that improve customer engagement, adoption, and retention
Design, maintain, and evolve scalable product KPIs and data models that provide consistent measurement across products
Build self-service dashboards, reports, and visualizations that enable teams to independently explore product performance while maintaining trusted business definitions
Conduct deep-dive analyses to understand product usage, customer journeys, and feature performance, translating findings into actionable recommendations
Develop hypotheses, evaluate product changes, and support experimentation and A/B testing where applicable
Partner with Data Engineering to improve event instrumentation, data quality, and analytics infrastructure
Communicate analytical findings to executive and cross-functional audiences through clear storytelling and data visualization
Improve analytics documentation, metric definitions, and best practices to promote consistency and scalability across teams


Minimum Qualifications


5+ years of experience in Product Analytics, Data Analytics, or Business Analytics, including experience influencing product or business decisions through data
Demonstrated experience presenting analytical findings and recommendations to senior leadership and executive audiences
Expertise in SQL and working with modern cloud data warehouse environments
Advanced experience building dashboards and visualizations using Power BI (required); experience with Tableau or Looker is a plus
Experience analyzing event-based product telemetry, clickstream, or user behavior datasets
Strong understanding of analytical methods, hypothesis-driven problem solving, and translating ambiguous business questions into measurable analyses
Experience working with modern analytics engineering and data pipeline technologies (DBT, Airflow, GitHub, or similar)
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience)


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with experimentation frameworks, A/B testing, or product measurement methodologies
Experience using Python (pandas, Jupyter, NumPy, or similar) for exploratory analysis and automation
Familiarity with product analytics platforms such as Amplitude, Mixpanel, Pendo, or similar tools
Experience integrating product analytics with Sales and Marketing data sources (Salesforce, Marketo, etc.)
Experience working within SaaS or cloud software organizations


Learn More

About Autodesk

Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made.

We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world.

When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us!

Salary transparency

Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. For Canada based roles, we expect a starting base salary between $107,000 and $156,200. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience and geographic location, and may exceed this range. In addition to base salaries, our compensation package may include annual cash bonuses, commissions for sales roles, stock grants, and a comprehensive benefits package.

Belonging

We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/global-belonging

Are you an existing contractor or consultant with Autodesk?

Please search for open jobs and apply internally (not on this external site).

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We’re searching for a Data Analyst. Are you searching for new possibilities?

Westland Insurance Group Ltd is growing and has an exciting opportunity for an Data Analyst to join our growing team. This hybrid role is based out of our Head Office in Surrey, BC.

Expected Compensation: The expected hiring range for this role is $62,400- $78,000 with a 37.5-hour work week. Your rate of pay will be determined based on your individual qualifications and experience, while considering internal equity. 

Westland also offers a competitive annual short-term incentive program that enables employees to share in the rewards of the business and emphasizes the measures that are key to our success.

Westland is committed to continuous learning where employees can grow their potential and write the stories of their careers. Financial assistance is available for approved work-related training/development courses leading to certification, and programs that offer professional development, including seminars and conferences.

Discover What’s Possible, With Westland.

The Westland story is all about growth, and that means plenty of possibility for everyone on our team. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of clients, communities, and each other with equal commitment. As we continue to open new doors, we're inviting amazing people like you to join us.

As a Data Analyst, you’ll have the unique opportunity to lay the foundation for how Westland can leverage data to make important decisions.

Interested in learning more?

Why You'll Love Westland


An open, flexible and welcoming workplace
Plenty of opportunities to grow and learn
Autonomy to drive your own success
Be part of our inclusive culture, alongside an extraordinarily talented community of people with a wide variety of backgrounds
A total rewards program that takes care of your financial, physical, and mental health
Flexible paid Values Days to celebrate days important to you
Paid Volunteer Days for employees as part of Amplifying Communities, a program to support causes that matter most to our employees and clients


Why We’ll Love You


You strive to understand the data and love seeking ways to improve the quality of the data and reporting
You enjoy collaborating with others to identify and prioritize use cases for analysis and build out models to answer business questions
You love numbers and have a strong background in math, statistics or equivalent such as a bachelor’s degree in those areas or related fields
You can translate complex information and findings for business leaders
At least two years’ experience in a data analytics role
Previous experience with data analysis and modeling using SQL, R, and Python
Bonus: Experience in P&C insurance
Bonus: Experience using data platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake or Microsoft Fabric Bonus: Experience with Git based version control on platforms such as GitHub or Azure DevOps


Once Here, You'll


Be a contributing member of our inclusive culture, alongside an extraordinarily talented community of people with a wide variety of backgrounds.
Working with IT and others to design and improve the quality of our data and reporting by understanding current and future data analysis needs, business opportunities and risks.
Continuously seek out and identify areas to improve data reporting practices, data quality, and processes
Prepare and present findings to diverse audiences, including partners


Want to get to know each other better? Send your resume our way.

You belong here

We strive to be much more than a great place to work. Here, you join a collective of incredibly vibrant, purpose driven, and open-minded people. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of communities, clients, and each other with equal commitment. You will be supported and respected for who you are and for the voice you add to the conversation.

At Westland, you will feel the power of community. Westland Insurance Ltd. is an inclusive organization that values diversity in its workforce. We listen, champion equity and diversity, and create safe welcoming spaces where everyone has a voice and is free to be themselves. Westland Insurance Ltd. encourages applications from all qualified individuals and will accommodate applicants' disability-related needs, up to the point of undue hardship, throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process.

If you require a disability-related accommodation in order to participate in the recruitment process, please contact the recruitment team by email at careers@westlandinsurance.ca

Title: Decision Science Analyst
Description:
Come join Pattison Food Group Ltd., a Jim Pattison business, and Canada’s largest Western-based provider of food and health products.

Pattison Food Group Ltd. is a certified Great Place to Work® and consists of Save-On-Foods and other well-known grocery banners. With nearly 300 retail locations, we proudly employ nearly 30,000 team members.

We have an exciting opportunity for a full-time Decision Science Analyst to join our More Rewards team in Langley, BC.

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Customer Data & Analytics, you will be responsible for leveraging all aspects of data and analytics to support large-scale projects, experimentation, and continuous improvement initiatives that guide decision-making and deliver high-quality insights. In this role, you will support the development and management of customer data and insights to support the More Rewards Loyalty Program, Marketing, Retail Operations and Merchandising.

You will be responsible for:


Collaborating with stakeholders to understand business questions, processes and KPIs.
Formulating testable hypotheses aligned with business questions and conducting statistical experiments in a live environment.
Writing SQL scripts to automate data pipelines and workflows.
Using Python scripts in a Jupyter Notebook environment for exploratory data analysis, data wrangling and statistical testing.
Developing and maintaining dashboards and visualizations in Power BI to convey insights, report on KPIs and test results.
Learning cloud-based analytics environments and big data tools to build analytics workflows on large datasets.
Discovering unique and creative insights to identify new opportunities for driving business value.
Becoming an expert in the relevant datasets within your analytics domain.
Supporting the Data & Analytics team with key projects.



You have:


A degree in a STEM discipline with 3+ years of relevant experience in data and analytics.
Advanced Python coding skills and understanding of SQL.
Experience with additional programming languages is an asset.
Experience using experimental design principles such as A/B and multivariate testing and working with business stakeholders to formulate testable hypotheses.
An understanding of advanced analytics and data science methods, such as anomaly detection, forecasting, and supervised & unsupervised learning.
Working knowledge of cloud environments, such as Azure, GCP or AWS. Experience with big data tools such as Spark and Databricks is an asset.



You are:


A confident communicator, both verbally and written, with strong interpersonal skills and the ability to communicate a story with data and insights to non-technical stakeholders.
A problem solver with strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
Effective in task management and delivering high-quality work.
Capable of multitasking and thriving in a fast-paced environment.
Self-motivated with the ability to work independently and collaboratively.



What we offer:

In addition to a highly competitive salary, we offer a great range of benefits, a company pension plan, free parking, exclusive team member offers, and opportunities for development and career progression.

If you’re looking to join a team that puts people first, you’re in the right place. Apply online today!

Compensation Details:

$83,394.00 - $104,243.00

The compensation offered for this position will take into consideration location, education, skills, experience and other factors.

Title: Product Information Analyst
Description:
Your Opportunity at ARC’TERYX:




As a Product Information Analyst, you ensure product data is accurate, complete, and operationally ready across the merchandising lifecycle. You enable scalable data management and decision making by maintaining high-quality product information that translates business inputs into structured, system-ready data. This role is a critical connector across Design, Product Development, Merchandising and Supply Chain teams, ensuring product data flows seamlessly from concept to launch. As you evolve in role, you leverage the data entered to generate reporting and insights that inform the assortment and enable better business decisions.     




This role is based out of our North Vancouver office and is open to hybrid work. Candidates must be eligible to work in Canada.




 




Meet Your Future Team:




The Process Operations organization sits at the heart of the Arc’teryx end-to-end value chain, leading enterprise-wide alignment, advancing a culture of radical collaboration. The Product Data & Analytics team, a new function within Process Operations, is the central authority and gatekeeper for seasonal product data. We are responsible for transforming data management from a necessary task to an enterprise capability, resulting in a portfolio of data products that enable better decisions from concept to guest. We partner with Technology, Product Creation, Merchandising and Master Data teams to evolve our governance model, modernize tooling, and embed KPI-led performance management into our end-to-end value chain. You will be at the forefront of these efforts, helping to shape the vision, operating model and execution of our initiatives.   







If you were in the Product Information Analyst role now, here are some of the core activities you would be doing:



Participating in key Innovation to Customer (I2C) milestones between Design, Product Development, and Merchandising teams


Creating, maintaining, and validating Model and SKU-level data in Merchandising and PLM systems


Ensuring completeness and accuracy of product attributes across the full lifecycle - from raw materials to finished goods


Executing and auditing seasonal data loads and updates in alignment with key Design, Development, Merchandising, and Planning team milestones


Creating and enforcing data standards, naming conventions, and attribute definitions


Identifying, communicating, and resolving data inconsistencies, gaps, and errors


Partnering with cross-functional teams to improve data integrity and reduce rework


Collaborating with Product Line Managers, Regional Merchants, Product Developers and Business Planners to capture and maintain accurate product information


Surfacing and resolving anomalies and edge cases related to attribution, hierarchy, and key measures


Translating your category’s business processes, seasonal strategy, and product inputs into structured system data


Acting as a subject matter expert on product data and business processes by championing issues and opportunities between Product Creation, Operations, and Technology teams


Ensuring market readiness by ensuring product data is complete and system ready


Monitoring deadlines to ensure timely delivery of all data milestones


Identifying opportunities to streamline workflows and improve efficiency


Contributing to documentation, training, and continuous improvement initiatives


Supporting implementation of data governance frameworks and tooling enhancements








Here are some of the things you could be working on in the future:



Acting as a data steward for your assigned category, owning the quality, structure, and usability of product data


Briefing in requirements for enhancing our analytics data platform to serve use cases for reporting, self-service tools, pre-configured dashboards, and modern query interfaces


Researching, identifying, and translating offline product attribution to systems-ready definitions and partnering with Technology teams to enable and scale where appropriate


Contributing to the redesign of Merchandising hierarchies across ERP systems, attributes, and taxonomies to improve data quality, searchability and analytics usefulness


Using tools such as SQL, Python, Tableau, Power BI (DAX) and Excel to develop and test analytics solutions in a sandbox environment


Partnering with Technology teams to push the most useful solutions to production and champion tool adoption amongst your user base, through business-friendly documentation and ongoing training and education  


Developing and presenting Merchandising analytics education modules in partnership with your Process Operations functional leaders    








Are you our next Product Information Analyst?



You have a bachelor’s degree or the equivalent experience in Business, Information Systems, or a related field, with 2+ years’ experience in product data management, merchandising operations, retail, or a related function


You are familiar with managing business operations in Direct-to-Consumer Retail, Wholesale or E-com environments


You have a general knowledge of Merchandising, Buying or Open to Buy processes


You have deep expertise in Excel and familiarity with advanced analytics tools (SQL, Python, Tableau, DAX)


You are familiar with the product development process, inclusive of both technical and creative aspects 


You are comfortable working at all levels of leadership, able to facilitate, present ideas and engage in curiosity, across technical and non-technical stakeholders


You have a strong intellectual curiously and a continuous improvement mindset


You are proactive to identify inefficiencies and have a demonstrated ability to design scalable, sustainable solutions


You have a proven ability to synthesize and interpret data, spot trends, draw conclusions, develop recommendations, and influence action


You are comfortable with ambiguous, fast-growth, entrepreneurial environments


You thrive in ambiguous environments and bring structure to complexity


You have exceptional problem-solving skills with a strong attention to detail








Compensation:The salary range for this role is CAD$71,000 - CAD$93,000
















Total Rewards




Most new team members join between the start and midpoint of the range, where you can contribute right away while continuing to grow your craft, your collaborations, and your impact at Arc’teryx. This range reflects market alignment and the scope of the role. Individual pay is determined by your skills, experience, and level of responsibility. 










We’re committed to fair, equitable, and competitive pay, reviewed regularly to ensure internal alignment and market relevance. Transparency is part of how we follow through on our commitments and how we honour the value you bring to our team. 










In addition to base pay, Arc’teryx supports people through benefits designed to sustain both work and life: 












Health & wellbeing - Extended health, dental, and vision coverage, including mental health support, fertility benefits, gender-affirming care and a 24/7 Employee Assistance Program (EAP).


Financial wellbeing - RRSP matching and eligibility for Arc'teryx Annual Incentive Plan and access to Employee Stock Purchase program (ESPP) where applicable.


Time & Flexibility - Paid time off, wellness time, and No Wasted Day program (dedicated paid days to get outside & explore).


Family support - Parental leave top-up and a nesting period for new parents.


Growth, community & gear - Professional development opportunities, Arc'teryx Academies (outdoor skill-building events), Employee Belonging Councils, and access to employee discounts and Pro Deals (exclusive discounted pricing on gear). 











 *Eligibility for certain programs may vary by role. 








Equal Opportunity




 




Arc’teryx is committed to actively creating and fostering a culture of inclusivity where voices are heard, people are seen, and values are respected. We care about the uniqueness of our applicants, employees, and guests, and we do so in a safe space fueled by curiosity and acceptance.

HTML:
Your Opportunity at ARC’TERYX:


As a Product Information Analyst, you ensure product data is accurate, complete, and operationally ready across the merchandising lifecycle. You enable scalable data management and decision making by maintaining high-quality product information that translates business inputs into structured, system-ready data. This role is a critical connector across Design, Product Development, Merchandising and Supply Chain teams, ensuring product data flows seamlessly from concept to launch. As you evolve in role, you leverage the data entered to generate reporting and insights that inform the assortment and enable better business decisions.     


This role is based out of our North Vancouver office and is open to hybrid work. Candidates must be eligible to work in Canada.


 


Meet Your Future Team:


The Process Operations organization sits at the heart of the Arc’teryx end-to-end value chain, leading enterprise-wide alignment, advancing a culture of radical collaboration. The Product Data & Analytics team, a new function within Process Operations, is the central authority and gatekeeper for seasonal product data. We are responsible for transforming data management from a necessary task to an enterprise capability, resulting in a portfolio of data products that enable better decisions from concept to guest. We partner with Technology, Product Creation, Merchandising and Master Data teams to evolve our governance model, modernize tooling, and embed KPI-led performance management into our end-to-end value chain. You will be at the forefront of these efforts, helping to shape the vision, operating model and execution of our initiatives.   



If you were in the Product Information Analyst role now, here are some of the core activities you would be doing:

  • Participating in key Innovation to Customer (I2C) milestones between Design, Product Development, and Merchandising teams

  • Creating, maintaining, and validating Model and SKU-level data in Merchandising and PLM systems

  • Ensuring completeness and accuracy of product attributes across the full lifecycle - from raw materials to finished goods

  • Executing and auditing seasonal data loads and updates in alignment with key Design, Development, Merchandising, and Planning team milestones

  • Creating and enforcing data standards, naming conventions, and attribute definitions

  • Identifying, communicating, and resolving data inconsistencies, gaps, and errors

  • Partnering with cross-functional teams to improve data integrity and reduce rework

  • Collaborating with Product Line Managers, Regional Merchants, Product Developers and Business Planners to capture and maintain accurate product information

  • Surfacing and resolving anomalies and edge cases related to attribution, hierarchy, and key measures

  • Translating your category’s business processes, seasonal strategy, and product inputs into structured system data

  • Acting as a subject matter expert on product data and business processes by championing issues and opportunities between Product Creation, Operations, and Technology teams

  • Ensuring market readiness by ensuring product data is complete and system ready

  • Monitoring deadlines to ensure timely delivery of all data milestones

  • Identifying opportunities to streamline workflows and improve efficiency

  • Contributing to documentation, training, and continuous improvement initiatives

  • Supporting implementation of data governance frameworks and tooling enhancements



Here are some of the things you could be working on in the future:

  • Acting as a data steward for your assigned category, owning the quality, structure, and usability of product data

  • Briefing in requirements for enhancing our analytics data platform to serve use cases for reporting, self-service tools, pre-configured dashboards, and modern query interfaces

  • Researching, identifying, and translating offline product attribution to systems-ready definitions and partnering with Technology teams to enable and scale where appropriate

  • Contributing to the redesign of Merchandising hierarchies across ERP systems, attributes, and taxonomies to improve data quality, searchability and analytics usefulness

  • Using tools such as SQL, Python, Tableau, Power BI (DAX) and Excel to develop and test analytics solutions in a sandbox environment

  • Partnering with Technology teams to push the most useful solutions to production and champion tool adoption amongst your user base, through business-friendly documentation and ongoing training and education  

  • Developing and presenting Merchandising analytics education modules in partnership with your Process Operations functional leaders    



Are you our next Product Information Analyst?

  • You have a bachelor’s degree or the equivalent experience in Business, Information Systems, or a related field, with 2+ years’ experience in product data management, merchandising operations, retail, or a related function

  • You are familiar with managing business operations in Direct-to-Consumer Retail, Wholesale or E-com environments

  • You have a general knowledge of Merchandising, Buying or Open to Buy processes

  • You have deep expertise in Excel and familiarity with advanced analytics tools (SQL, Python, Tableau, DAX)

  • You are familiar with the product development process, inclusive of both technical and creative aspects 

  • You are comfortable working at all levels of leadership, able to facilitate, present ideas and engage in curiosity, across technical and non-technical stakeholders

  • You have a strong intellectual curiously and a continuous improvement mindset

  • You are proactive to identify inefficiencies and have a demonstrated ability to design scalable, sustainable solutions

  • You have a proven ability to synthesize and interpret data, spot trends, draw conclusions, develop recommendations, and influence action

  • You are comfortable with ambiguous, fast-growth, entrepreneurial environments

  • You thrive in ambiguous environments and bring structure to complexity

  • You have exceptional problem-solving skills with a strong attention to detail



Compensation:The salary range for this role is CAD$71,000 - CAD$93,000






Total Rewards


Most new team members join between the start and midpoint of the range, where you can contribute right away while continuing to grow your craft, your collaborations, and your impact at Arc’teryx. This range reflects market alignment and the scope of the role. Individual pay is determined by your skills, experience, and level of responsibility. 




We’re committed to fair, equitable, and competitive pay, reviewed regularly to ensure internal alignment and market relevance. Transparency is part of how we follow through on our commitments and how we honour the value you bring to our team. 




In addition to base pay, Arc’teryx supports people through benefits designed to sustain both work and life: 





  • Health & wellbeing - Extended health, dental, and vision coverage, including mental health support, fertility benefits, gender-affirming care and a 24/7 Employee Assistance Program (EAP).

  • Financial wellbeing - RRSP matching and eligibility for Arc'teryx Annual Incentive Plan and access to Employee Stock Purchase program (ESPP) where applicable.

  • Time & Flexibility - Paid time off, wellness time, and No Wasted Day program (dedicated paid days to get outside & explore).

  • Family support - Parental leave top-up and a nesting period for new parents.

  • Growth, community & gear - Professional development opportunities, Arc'teryx Academies (outdoor skill-building events), Employee Belonging Councils, and access to employee discounts and Pro Deals (exclusive discounted pricing on gear). 




 *Eligibility for certain programs may vary by role.

Title: Data Analyst, Player & Quality Insights
Description:
Description & Requirements

Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.

The Player & Quality Insights team focuses on understanding player behavior and designing player feedback events which help game teams develop high-quality games to meet our player's expectations. We are a data-driven team where every major decision starts with data analysis to find opportunity areas and to measure our efforts. As a Data Analyst, you will oversee and conduct the conversion of data into insights to lead to informed decisions. Reporting to a Manager, Analytics, you will develop new methods to understand our players and directly influence the quality of our games.

Responsibilities


Work with partners to understand project goals and provide clear guidance regarding player behavior and player feedback event design.
Evaluate and interpret data to find risks and opportunities and communicate them with your team and partners.
Build statistical models and validate and improve the performance of these models over time.
Develop scripts and queries to import, clean, transform, and enhance data.
Prepare detailed reports and presentations that help your team identify areas of opportunity and provide insight to guide future playtest events
Promote best practices in tools and visual technologies for building project dashboards that provide important data for project monitoring.


Qualifications


2+ years of work experience in a past analytical role, using data to guide process improvements.
Bachelor's degree in a technical field or completion of post-secondary level Math and Statistics courses.
Excellent foundation of math and statistical knowledge including an understanding of probability, correlation, regression, classification, and the development of statistical models.
Proficient in SQL and ability to extract data from large data sources with differing structures.
Proficient in software tools like Power BI or Tableau to create easy-to-understand data visualizations.
Experience with the Agile/Waterfall processes.
Experience presenting and prioritizing with people at multiple levels of business and technical expertise.


Pay Transparency - North America

Compensation And Benefits

The ranges listed below are what EA in good faith expects to pay applicants for this role in these locations at the time of this posting. If you reside in a different location, a recruiter will advise on the applicable range and benefits. Pay offered will be determined based on a number of relevant business and candidate factors (e.g. education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, or business needs).

PAY RANGES


British Columbia (depending on location e.g. Vancouver vs. Victoria) *$77,700 - $107,900 CAD


Pay is just one part of the overall compensation at EA.

For Canada, we offer a package of benefits including vacation (3 weeks per year to start), 10 days per year of sick time, paid top-up to EI/QPIP benefits up to 100% of base salary when you welcome a new child (12 weeks for maternity, and 4 weeks for parental/adoption leave), extended health/dental/vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, retirement plan to regular full-time employees. Certain roles may also be eligible for bonus and equity.

About Electronic Arts

We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.

Electronic Arts is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, genetic information, religion, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status, family status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will also consider employment qualified applicants with criminal records in accordance with applicable law. EA also makes workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law.

Title: Data Developer
Description:
Your Job

DarkVision, a Koch Engineered Solutions company, is looking for a Data (Python) Developer to join the Data Development Team in North Vancouver, BC. In this role, you will work collaboratively with teammates and other departments in a fast-paced, ever-changing environment to develop and advance the data analysis deliverables supporting the multi-million-dollar operations of our customers.




You will support the Data Analysis Delivery team by writing and maintaining scripts to automate their analysis and deliverable workflows. You will work with the Product team to prototype new deliverables (inspection reports and 3D visualizations), and work with the Engineering Team to diagnose issues that affect Data Analysis Deliverables, and suggest improvements.




This regular full-time position is 100% on-site in our HQ in North Vancouver, BC, where employees enjoy full access to our facility amenities including a well-equipped gym, squash court, climbing wall, steam room, and more!




Immigration sponsorship or support is not available for this position.




Our Team

DarkVision’s acoustic imaging technology captures terabytes of high-fidelity data with each run. Our Data Analysis Development team plays a critical role in the commercial process by supporting the Data Analysis Delivery team in building and maintaining tools to streamline the analysis workflow, advancing and improving new deliverables, and improving processes for quality assurance.




There will always be new software tools and scripts to write and improve, new technologies to exploit and generate deliverables on, and new ways to make processes better, faster, and more sustainable and scalable. This entails a detail-oriented mindset, a default-to-action work ethic, and a multi-disciplinary perspective.




What You Will Do

Develop and maintain scripts to automate analysis workflows.
Develop and maintain analysis dashboards for higher level tracking of project status and Key Performance Indicators.
Develop and maintain analysis procedures that streamline a sustainable and scalable workflow.
Work with other analysts, Sales, Product, and Engineering teams to document and investigate issues, and prevent their reoccurrence.
Gather and analyze test data that will be used to prove out feasibility of new technology and deliverable products.
Develop new and innovative ways of representing and visualizing current data to generate new customer deliverables.
Work with the Product team in the data gathering, analysis, and writing of technical conferences papers.
Help analysts debug issues in their analysis workflow, and reproduce, document, and raise issues for other functional teams.
Interface with our Research, Machine Learning, Software, and Product teams to ensure the Data Team’s needs are met, and vice versa.




Who You Are (Basic Qualifications)

Able to work 100% on-site in our North Vancouver office.
Experience developing production code in Python (code used in a product or professional setting, not academia- or research-only applications).
Experience developing workflow automation scripts (to automate manual steps performed by analysts).
Data visualization experience.
Git experience.




What Will Put You Ahead

Excellent communication skills.
Detail oriented; meticulous in documentation.
Default-to-action personality.
Independent - can operate with little guidance while providing results at regular intervals for feedback.
Collaborative - works to meet the needs and goals of the team.
Image processing experience.
MATLAB experience.
3D data experience.
3D rendering experience.
Proficiency in PowerBI.
Ultrasonic imaging experience.
Software and algorithm development experience.
Atlassian product experience.




General Salary Range

For this role, we anticipate paying $70,000 - $115,000 per year. This role is eligible for variable pay, issued as a monetary bonus or in another form.




At Koch companies, we are entrepreneurs. This means we openly challenge the status quo, find new ways to create value and get rewarded for our individual contributions. Any compensation range provided for a role is an estimate determined by available market data. The actual amount may be higher or lower than the range provided considering each candidate’s knowledge, skills, abilities, and geographic location. If you have questions, please speak to your recruiter about the flexibility and detail of our compensation philosophy.




Who We Are

DarkVision Technologies Inc. is a Canada-based tech company disrupting the industrial imaging market since 2013. We have created the world’s most advanced acoustic-based imaging platform. We are packaging it into multiple new product lines, revolutionizing how our clients quantify and visualize the integrity of their critical assets.




Backed by Koch Industries, one of the world’s largest privately held companies, DarkVision’s team of Mechanical, Skunkworks, Electrical, Software, and Machine Learning Engineers is rapidly expanding to meet the demand for the company’s current and upcoming products.




We allow employees to work on cutting-edge technologies that blend science with real-world applications. We invite you to join our team for the exciting journey ahead as we become the global leader in industrial imaging.




At Koch, employees are empowered to do what they do best to make life better. Learn how our business philosophy helps employees unleash their potential while creating value for themselves and the company.




Additionally, everyone has individual work and personal needs. We seek to enable the best work environment that helps you and the business work together to produce superior results.




How To Apply

If you have the above qualifications, we would like to hear from you. We thank all applicants in advance, but please be advised that only those selected for an interview will be contacted.




We are an equal opportunity employer. If you require accommodation or assistance at any time during the application or selection processes, please submit a request by following the directions located in the FAQ section at the bottom of the kochcareers.com webpage.




Successful candidates will be required to complete a criminal background check.




keywords: Python, workflow automation, automate, analysis, analyses, analytics, data, deliverables, reports, reporting, development, developer, programming, scripting, visualization, 3D, image processing, MATLAB, Atlassian, PowerBI

Title: Data Analyst II, Fraud
Description:
Job Description:

At Remitly, we believe everyone deserves the freedom to access, move, and manage their money wherever life takes them. Since 2011, we've tirelessly delivered on our promise to customers sending money globally, providing secure, simple, and reliable ways to manage their money, ensuring true peace of mind.

Whether it's supporting loved ones back home, growing a business across continents, or pursuing new opportunities abroad, we're not just here to move money— we're here to move our global customers forward.

We're looking for builders, reimaginers, and global thinkers who want to work at the intersection of technology, trust, and transformation. If that's you and you're ready to do the most meaningful work of your career—we invite you to join over 2,800 passionate Remitlians worldwide who are united by our vision to transform lives with trusted financial services that transcend borders

About the Role:

As a Data Analyst, Fraud, you will be part of Remitly's Identity & Trust Analytics Team. Your mission is to leverage analytical methods to identify, prevent, and mitigate fraud impacting our customers globally. You will develop a deep understanding of Remitly's fraud detection systems, regional risk profiles, and operational processes to improve fraud defenses while maintaining a seamless experience for legitimate customers. You'll partner with Product, Engineering, Business Management, and Compliance to customize and optimize fraud models and workflows, integrating data from legacy and new platforms. This hybrid role requires 2–3 days per week in the Vancouver office.

You Will:


Become an expert on Remitly's fraud data (e.g. chargebacks, promo abuse, scams, onboarding risk).
Build data-driven models and dashboards to analyze and monitor fraud exposure, balancing customer experience with operational efficiency.
Collect, normalize, and analyze data from legacy prebriefs, transaction logs, and onboarding systems to identify fraud trends and behavior anomalies.
Partner with Business Management and Product to continuously improve customer verification, KYC, KYB and compliance workflows.
Design and evaluate experiments and A/B tests to measure fraud control effectiveness and minimize false positives.
Develop and track risk metrics that quantify fraud exposure, policy compliance, and system performance.
Provide actionable insights and influence the development of fraud detection strategies, tools, and automation.


You Have:


3+ years in an analytics or fraud/risk analysis role within fintech, e-commerce, or digital payments.
Experience with fraud or trust analytics.
Bachelor's degree in mathematics, engineering, economics, or another quantitative discipline (or equivalent experience).
Proficiency with SQL and hands-on experience analyzing large datasets (Python, R, or statistical modeling experience preferred).
Strong understanding of fraud taxonomies (chargebacks, identity theft, scams, onboarding risk, and promo abuse).
Demonstrated ability to distill business problems, develop hypotheses, perform analysis, and communicate clear recommendations.
Excellent data storytelling and communication skills; ability to translate complex findings into business decisions.


Compensation Details. The starting base salary range for this position is $100,000 - $125000. In Canada, Remitly employees are shareholders in our Company and equity is part of our total compensation plan. Your recruiter can share more information about medical benefits offered, as well as other financial benefits and total compensation components offered with this role.

Our Benefits


Four weeks vacation
Health Benefits
Mental Health & Family Forming Benefits
RRSP plan with company match
Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)
Life Insurance & Disability
Continuing education and travel benefits


Our Connected Work Culture: Driving Innovation, Together

At Remitly, we believe that true innovation sparks when we come together. Our Connected Work Culture fosters dynamic in-person collaboration, where ideas ignite and challenging problems find solutions faster. For corporate team members, we have an in-office expectation of at least 50% of the time monthly, typically achieved by coming in three days a week. This creates a consistent, meaningful overlap that supports team norms and business needs. Managers also have the flexibility to set higher expectations based on their team's specific needs. These intentional in-office moments are vital for deepening relationships, fueling creativity, and ensuring your impact is felt where it matters most.

Remitly is an E-Verify Employer

At Remitly, we are dedicated to ensuring that our workplace offers equal employment opportunities to all employees and candidates, in full compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

Remitly is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

If that's you and you're ready to do the most meaningful work of your career—we invite you to join over 2,800 passionate Remitlians worldwide who are united by our vision to transform lives with trusted financial services that transcend borders

About the Role:

As a Data Analyst, Fraud, you will be part of Remitly's Identity & Trust Analytics Team. Your mission is to leverage analytical methods to identify, prevent, and mitigate fraud impacting our customers globally. You will develop a deep understanding of Remitly's fraud detection systems, regional risk profiles, and operational processes to improve fraud defenses while maintaining a seamless experience for legitimate customers. You'll partner with Product, Engineering, Business Management, and Compliance to customize and optimize fraud models and workflows, integrating data from legacy and new platforms. This hybrid role requires 2–3 days per week in the Vancouver office.

You Will:

  • Become an expert on Remitly's fraud data (e.g. chargebacks, promo abuse, scams, onboarding risk).
  • Build data-driven models and dashboards to analyze and monitor fraud exposure, balancing customer experience with operational efficiency.
  • Collect, normalize, and analyze data from legacy prebriefs, transaction logs, and onboarding systems to identify fraud trends and behavior anomalies.
  • Partner with Business Management and Product to continuously improve customer verification, KYC, KYB and compliance workflows.
  • Design and evaluate experiments and A/B tests to measure fraud control effectiveness and minimize false positives.
  • Develop and track risk metrics that quantify fraud exposure, policy compliance, and system performance.
  • Provide actionable insights and influence the development of fraud detection strategies, tools, and automation.

You Have:

  • 3+ years in an analytics or fraud/risk analysis role within fintech, e-commerce, or digital payments.
  • Experience with fraud or trust analytics.
  • Bachelor's degree in mathematics, engineering, economics, or another quantitative discipline (or equivalent experience).
  • Proficiency with SQL and hands-on experience analyzing large datasets (Python, R, or statistical modeling experience preferred).
  • Strong understanding of fraud taxonomies (chargebacks, identity theft, scams, onboarding risk, and promo abuse).
  • Demonstrated ability to distill business problems, develop hypotheses, perform analysis, and communicate clear recommendations.
  • Excellent data storytelling and communication skills; ability to translate complex findings into business decisions.

Compensation Details. The starting base salary range for this position is $100,000 - $125000. In Canada, Remitly employees are shareholders in our Company and equity is part of our total compensation plan. Your recruiter can share more information about medical benefits offered, as well as other financial benefits and total compensation components offered with this role.

Our Benefits

  • Four weeks vacation
  • Health Benefits
  • Mental Health & Family Forming Benefits
  • RRSP plan with company match
  • Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)
  • Life Insurance & Disability
  • Continuing education and travel benefits

Our Connected Work Culture: Driving Innovation, Together

At Remitly, we believe that true innovation sparks when we come together. Our Connected Work Culture fosters dynamic in-person collaboration, where ideas ignite and challenging problems find solutions faster. For corporate team members, we have an in-office expectation of at least 50% of the time monthly, typically achieved by coming in three days a week. This creates a consistent, meaningful overlap that supports team norms and business needs. Managers also have the flexibility to set higher expectations based on their team's specific needs. These intentional in-office moments are vital for deepening relationships, fueling creativity, and ensuring your impact is felt where it matters most.

Remitly is an E-Verify Employer

At Remitly, we are dedicated to ensuring that our workplace offers equal employment opportunities to all employees and candidates, in full compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

Remitly is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Title: Data Analyst - Switchgear
Description:
At IEM, we’re not just building innovative electrical distribution systems, we’re shaping the future. IEM is dedicated to delivering world-class solutions for complex power needs. After 75 years, we continue to push the boundaries of what’s possible. Whether you’re an experienced professional or just starting out, you’ll have the opportunity to contribute, grow, and make a lasting impact on industries that power the world’s most dynamic markets.

Position Summary

The Switchgear Data Analyst plays a critical role in enabling data-driven decision making across the switchgear assembly operations. This role owns the collection, analysis, and visualization of Switchgear performance data, including throughput, utilization/downtimes, reworks, labor efficiency, and capacity. The analyst partners closely with the Director of Switchgear, Operations Managers, quality team and the Switchgear Engineering team to support process implementation, standardization, and control. This role ensures that daily data are translated into actionable insights that drive throughput, cost reduction, and continuous improvement in a timely manner.

Please note: This position requires 100% in-office presence in our Surrey offices and does not offer remote work.

Key Responsibilities


Collect, clean, validate, and analyze Switchgear data from OEE, MES, ERP (SyteLine), and manual sources to generate accurate performance insights.
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports using Power BI, Tableau, or similar tools, providing daily, weekly, and monthly visibility into production performance.
Monitor and evaluate KPIs including on time start, production plan attainment, utilization, reworks, throughput, labor efficiency, and capacity.
Support the Switchgear leadership team in identifying bottlenecks, trends, and systemic issues.
Provide detailed root-cause data analysis for downtime, quality issues, delays, or process instability.
Participate in process implementation and process control activities, including validation of standard work, cycle times, routings, load balancing, and line balancing.
Assist with configuration and optimization of MES to ensure accurate data capture and alignment with real workflows.
Support the rollout of new process standards, automation cells, and engineering changes by providing required data analysis and control tools.
Collaborate with Planning on capacity modeling, production forecasting, and scenario simulations for load balancing across work centers.
Create automated reporting systems and alert mechanisms to flag performance risks to Operations Managers and the Director of Switchgear.
Contribute to continuous improvement projects, supporting CI teams with data modeling, statistical analysis, and project tracking.
Ensure data integrity, documentation consistency, and alignment with company-wide reporting standards.


Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in engineering, Industrial Engineering, Data Analytics, Manufacturing Technology, or related technical field.
2–5 years of experience in manufacturing analytics, operations analysis, industrial engineering, or similar role.
Strong experience with ERP systems (Infor SyteLine preferred).
Advanced proficiency in Power BI, Tableau, Excel, and data visualization tools.
Strong skills in SQL, data manipulation, and database structures are an asset.
Understanding of manufacturing processes, preferably in Switchgear (mechanical and electrical assembly).
Familiarity with OEE, MES, single piece flow and assembly line monitoring systems is preferred.
Experience supporting process implementation, process control, or standard work documentation is an asset.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into clear recommendations and actions.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to influence operational leaders through data storytelling.


Compensation

Compensation offered for this role will take into account internal equity and also may vary depending on the candidate's geographic region, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience among other factors. A bonus and/or long-term incentive units may be provided as part of the compensation package, in addition to the full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits, depending on the level and position offered.

Base Compensation Range: CAD 41.82 – 53.37/hr

Why Join IEM

At IEM, you’ll join a team that powers some of the world’s most ambitious projects. We’re engineers, makers, and problem-solvers who thrive on tackling complex challenges and delivering solutions that keep industries moving forward. If you’re driven, collaborative, and ready to make an impact, we’d love to hear from you. Your creativity and passion can help us achieve great things—come be part of the journey.

Learn more about IEM at https://www.iemfg.com

We offer comprehensive and competitive benefits package designed to support our employees' well-being, growth, and long-term success. View a snapshot of our benefits at https://www.iemfg.com/careers

Recruiting Scams

Beware of recruiting scams. IEM never charges candidates fees, and all recruiter emails come from an @iemfg.com address. If you suspect fraudulent activity, do not share personal information and report it to us at iemfg.com/contact

Non-Discrimination Statement

IEM does not discriminate against any applicant based on any characteristic protected by law.

Privacy

Information collected and processed as part of your IEM Careers profile, and any job applications you choose to submit is subject to IEM's Workforce Member Privacy Policy.

Title: Data Analyst, Investment Data Management
Description:
Interested in joining one of Canada’s top performing asset managers? We are looking for a Data Analyst for an existing vacancy on our Investment Data Management (IDM) team. The IDM Team resides within the middle office and is responsible for managing data centric processes and data quality that supports the investment management business. Connor, Clark & Lunn Financial Group Ltd. (CCLFG) manages a wide range of global assets and heavily relies on the output of the Data Management Team for a variety of functions from the front office to the back. This is a fantastic opportunity to be part of a dynamic strategic team whose scope and importance continues to grow enterprise wide. We place a high value on maintaining an entrepreneurial spirit and creating a culture where each of us has opportunities to succeed. We are a solution orientated and collaborative team seeking like-minded individuals who thrive in a challenging and dynamic environment. Our offices currently operate on a hybrid model with a combination of in-office and remote working days throughout the week.

What You Will Do


Maintain and validate accuracy of equity/fixed income/derivative data across multiple systems/databases to enable Investment Manager modeling, trading, and risk monitoring.
Manage and enable a myriad of scheduled processes for vendor systems, internal and market data transfers, valuation, and benchmarking data inputs to the investment process.
Deliver timely data signoffs to investment teams; develop and analyze data-quality exceptions and ensure timely resolution.
Provide analysis on operational needs, ad-hoc data/data-history requests for multiple business partners to support pre- and post-trade portfolio management functions.
Identify process/workflow improvements to increase operational efficiency, mitigate risk, improve data-quality, and increase service levels provided to stakeholders.
Supporter of other operational or engineering staff, senior or managers on a variety of ad-hoc tasks and projects.


What You Bring


You have a degree in Finance, Commerce, Business Administration, Business Operations, or a related field. Relevant designation, such as the MBA, CFA, or FRM is an asset.
2-6 years of relevant experience in the investment management industry with exposure to securities data, data quality and process management. Hands-on experience working with Bloomberg, FTSE, ICE Bond Edge, Charles River and related financial tools, platforms is an asset
You possess an analytical mindset and able to break down complex processes into components to logically deduce the root cause of issues in data and technology.
You have a collaborative spirit that helps you to work well with others and you communicate effectively to team/cross-team members, and upward to key stakeholders.
You have a passion for advancing the quality, scale, and efficiency of data processes and can take ownership of multiple processes with keen attention to detail.
You constantly take a critical eye to processes with the goal of finding improvement opportunities and bring an ability to manage multiple projects.


The salary range for this position is $60,000-$90,000. The salary range provided reflects the base salary range for this position as required by legislation. In addition, there is an annual performance bonus which contributes to the total compensation for this position. Further questions may be directed to the HR team during the interview process.

  

For a closer look at how you can build your career with us, we invite you to explore cclgroup.com. 

CC&L Financial Group is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation or expression, disability, or age. 

Your application will be reviewed by a member of the hiring team - AI is not used in the screening, assessment or selection of applications at this time.

Title: Continuous Improvement Analyst
Description:
S'more About The Team

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Operational Excellence (OpEx), you will support OpEx teams in delivering continuous improvement projects across distribution center operations, from defining opportunities, data analysis, solution design, implementation and process change management. As a part of the OpEx team, you will play a key role in improving our operational quality, efficiency & waste, and help us deliver the best possible value to our customers by developing a deep understanding of our processes, data and technology.

Lettuce Share What This Role Will Be Responsible For


Drive the Operational Excellence vision of continuously improving quality, efficiency and engagement
Quickly understand and communicate complex processes to others
Take ownership of core warehouse and production processes by driving standardization and continuous improvement
Act as a champion of site operations, working closely with stakeholders both on the shop floor and in the office alike
Leverage large internal datasets to drive decisions, identify and quantify opportunities, and prioritize to deliver improvements in cost & quality
Support root cause and failure mode & effects analyses to mitigate risks and prevent recurring issues related to product quality, pick and pack accuracy, minute-per-box efficiency, and waste
Support the development and delivery of communication and change management plans to relevant stakeholders to identify and remove project implementation risks and effectively launch and sustain operational changes
Create standard operating procedures, work instruction documentation and other visual management tools
Track and provide project progress updates in the OpEx Roadmap
Use personal protective and safety equipment and clothing as directed by the employer and report workplace hazards and dangers to the supervisor or employer.
Other duties, as assigned.


Sound a-peeling? Here's what we're looking for:


Bachelor’s degree in business, engineering, supply chain, or a similar field
2+ years of experience working in operations management or a related field
A natural curiosity to understand how different systems/processes work and a bias for action to improve them
A proactive and ambitious mindset: you take ownership of your work and think beyond the scope of a given assignment
A charismatic attitude to getting buy-in on your ideas from the design stage to implementation on the shop floor
Quantitative and analytical experience (either through education or professional experience), including expertise with G-Suite, Excel, and data visualization systems
Experience with AI tools is a plus
A willingness to travel within Canada up to 15% of your working time
You are highly interested in learning about or already have experience in:
Root Cause Analysis tools: 5 Whys, Fault Tree Analysis, 6W2H, etc.
Familiarity with Lean/Business Process Mapping
Basic coding and/or data visualization (e.g. SQL, Tableau). If you know how to write code and pull data, that’s awesome. If not, don’t worry as long as you have an interest in it and aptitude for learning it.

Let’s cut to the cheese, this is why you'll love it here:


Box Discount - Amazing discounts on 1 box per week! 75% discount on weekly HelloFresh and Chefs Plate meal kits AND 50% off weekly Factor meal box.
Health & Wellness - Health & Dental benefits from day 1, a Health Spending Account, unlimited access to the Headspace app to meet your self-care needs, and 25% discount on GoodLife fitness memberships!
RRSP Matching - Competitive RRSP matching program offered
Vacation & PTO - Time off is also an important part of self-care! We offer generous vacation and PTO to help you create a good work-life balance.
Family Benefits - A parental leave top-up program for expectant parents.
Growth & Development - We support your career progression, provide development opportunities, and invest in your continued learning through our organization wide L&D fund.
Work Hard & Have Fun - From team socials to engaging company days, you’ll have plenty of opportunity to experience the fun!
Diversity & Inclusion Initiatives - With impactful ERG’s like FreshPride, Women Empowered and LIMES, we are committed to our diversity, equity & inclusion efforts.
Food Puns - this one is kind of a big dill if you haven’t already noticed. We even have some punny meeting room names!


Working Conditions

It’s no surprise that as a food company, we use many ingredients that may be considered common allergens (i.e. peanuts, tree nuts, milk, etc.). Whether this role is based out of our headquarters or one of our distribution centers, it is possible that you may be exposed to such allergens in this role. If you have any concerns with being exposed to any particular food ingredients, including meat, please disclose this during the hiring process.

If this sounds like you, please click the "Apply Now" button. If this role doesn't quite match what you're looking for, but you'd like to know more about joining the HelloFresh team, please visit our Jobs page to view our other vacancies. We'd love to hear from you!

This includes tools that screen and assess candidate qualifications based on the requirements for this role. While these tools assist our workflow, all final selection decisions are made by our hiring team.

This is a posting for an existing vacancy. We are actively seeking to fill this position.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Are you a proactive Senior Data Analyst skilled in analytics, from data transformation to statistical analysis and machine learning? Do you thrive on solving problems and driving innovation? If you’re eager to turn insights into action and empower others, join us to shape the future of data-driven decision-making in a collaborative, forward-thinking team. In this key role, you'll work with business leaders and cross-functional teams to harness data, deliver actionable insights, and drive strategy, smarter decisions, and innovation.

We are currently hiring for a 12-month fixed-term opportunity within our Data Analytics Team.

We practice a remote first working model, leveraging in person interactions at our head office, in beautiful Vancouver, BC, for strategic, collaborative and social purposes.

Apply now and grow your career with Best Buy as a Senior Data Analyst.

What You’ll Do As a Senior Data Analyst


Be the go-to data expert for business decision support: Conduct problem research, deep-dive analysis, apply advanced analytics and ML techniques to uncover trends and insights, design high-impact reports and visuals to tell compelling data stories that inspire action and shape our offerings and operations.
Lead the way on strategic projects: Tackle complex challenges and enable data solutions using standard and advanced analytics, automation, business intelligence, opportunity analysis, forecasting, and simulations.
Innovate for tomorrow: Design and implement experiments that directly influence our customer experience, helping us stay ahead of the curve. Spot new analytics opportunities that propel our business forward, improve our practices to increase business outcomes and be the catalyst for positive change.
Build the tools of transformation: Help us get to the future by gradually developing and maintaining scalable reporting and machine learning models that empower business and tech teams with self-serve access to actionable descriptive and predictive insights.
Become a trusted advisor: Build a deep understanding of needs and goals of our stakeholders, help see possibilities, guide through complexity, influence decisions and develop data solutions to help business succeed.
Champion for growth and collaboration: Nurture the analytics community, help establish new standards, best practices and frameworks, mentor and inspire fellow analysts by sharing knowledge, peer reviews, and brainstorming sessions.


What We’re Looking For


A minimum of 4+ years of senior analytics experience executing enterprise analytics initiatives, managing data transformation, automation, analysis, reporting, ML simulations, and business insights in a retail setting.
Mastery of analytics tools and techniques to drive results using SQL (including optimization), Python (data prep and wrangling, analysis, visualization, ML, automation), and Power BI (data modeling, DAX, M, dashboard UX design).
Experience breaking down complex business problems into actionable data solutions using statistical methods, modeling, or machine learning.
Experience partnering with diverse stakeholders where you make complex data concepts clear and engaging for everyone from executives to frontline teams.
